blocks:
Sūrat al-Anfāl

It is Medinan, according to Ibn Abbas and Qatadah, except for seven verses that were revealed in Mecca: وَإِذْ يَمْكُرُ بِكَ الَّذِينَ (8:30) to the end of them. And it is said that it was revealed entirely concerning the Battle of Badr.

The number of its verses is seventy-five verses. بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(1) يَسْئَلُونَكَ عَنِ الْأَنْفَالِ (They question you concerning the spoils of war) concerning its ruling (حكمها), and they are specific spoils (غنائم خاصّة). And al-nafl (النّفل) is the increase upon a thing. The spoils (الغنيمة) were named by it because they are a gift (عَطيّة) from Allah and a favor (فضل).

In al-Majma', it is narrated that al-Sajjad, al-Baqir, and al-Sadiq (peace be upon them) read: يَسْئَلُونَكَ الْأَنْفَال (They question you [for] the spoils), meaning that you should give them [the spoils]. قُلِ الْأَنْفَالُ لِلَّهِ وَالرَّسُولِ (Say, 'The spoils belong to Allah and the Apostle'), meaning they are exclusive to both of Them; They place them wherever They wish.

In al-Tahdhib, narrated from al-Baqir and al-Sadiq (peace be upon them): Al-Fay' (الفيء) and الْأَنْفَالِ are what came from land upon which no blood was shed, or a people (1) who made peace and surrendered willingly, and what came from ruined land (أرض خربة) or the bottoms of valleys (بطون أودية). All of this is from al-Fay' (الفيء) and al-Anfal (الأنفال). So, all of this belongs to Allah and His Apostle. Whatever belongs to Allah belongs to His Apostle; He places it wherever He wishes, and it belongs to the Imam after the Apostle.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): الْأَنْفَالِ is that upon which neither horses nor riding beasts were spurred, or a people who made peace, or a people who surrendered willingly. And every ruined land (أرض خربة) and the bottoms of valleys (بطون الأودية) belong to the Apostle of Allah, and they belong to the Imam after him; he places them wherever he wishes.

And narrated from him (peace be upon him) in several reports: Whoever dies and has no heir, his wealth is from al-Anfal (الْأَنْفَالِ).

And narrated from him (peace be upon him): We are a people whose obedience Allah has made obligatory; al-Anfal (الْأَنْفَالِ) belongs to us, and the choicest part (صفو) (1) of the wealth belongs to us.

And al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): الْأَنْفَالِ belongs to us. It was asked: "And what is al-Anfal (الأنفال)?" He said: "Among it are the mines (المعادن), the thickets (الآجام), and every land that has no owner (ربّ لها), and every land whose inhabitants have perished (باد أهلها), belongs to us." And he said: "What belonged to the kings (للملوك) is from al-Anfal (الأنفال)."

And in al-Jawami', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): الْأَنْفَالِ is everything taken from the Abode of War (دار الحرب) without fighting, and every land whose inhabitants evacuated without fighting—which the jurists (الفقهاء) called fay' (فيئاً)—and the barren lands (الأرضون الموات), the thickets (الآجام), the bottoms of valleys (بطون الأودية), the estates of the kings (قطايع الملوك), and the inheritance of one who has no heir. And it belongs to Allah and the Apostle, and to whoever stands in His place after Him.

And al-Qummi narrated from him (peace be upon him): That he was asked about الْأَنْفَالِ, and he said: "They are the villages (القرى) that have been ruined and whose inhabitants have evacuated, and they belong to Allah and the Apostle. And what belonged to the kings (للملوك) belongs to the Imam, and what came from ruined land (أرض خربة) upon which neither horses nor riding beasts were spurred (لم يوجف)..."

footnote (١) Clarification: The phrase "or a people" (*أو قوم*) in the two places is understood with an implied addition (*بتقدير مضاف*), and it is an example of joining the specific (*الخاص*) to the general (*العام*), because the first [category] includes what its inhabitants evacuated from.

(٢) upon which neither horses nor riding beasts were spurred, and every land that has no owner (ربّ). And the minerals (المعادن) are from it, and whoever dies having no heir (مولىً), then his property (ما له) is part of al-anfal. And He said: It was revealed on the Day of Badr when the people were defeated. The companions of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم were divided into three groups (فرق): one group remained near the tent of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, another group raided for plunder (النهب), and a third group pursued the enemy, took captives (أسروا), and acquired spoils (غنموا). When they gathered the spoils (الغنائم) and the captives (الأسارى), the Ansar spoke concerning the captives, so Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, revealed: ما كانَ لِنَبِيٍّ أَنْ يَكُونَ لَهُ أَسْرى حَتَّى يُثْخِنَ فِي الْأَرْضِ ** (It is not for a prophet to have captives until he has thoroughly subdued the land.) When Allah permitted the captives and the spoils to them, Sa'd ibn Mu'adh spoke—and he was among those who remained near the tent of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. He said: "O Messenger of Allah, what prevented us from pursuing the enemy was neither lack of desire (زهادة) for Jihad nor cowardice (جبناً) toward the enemy, but we feared that your position would be exposed, allowing the cavalry of the polytheists to attack you. The leaders (وجُوه) of the Muhajirun and the Ansar remained near the tent, and none of them doubted. O Messenger of Allah, the people are numerous, but the spoils are few. And if you give to these (who fought), nothing will remain for your companions." And he feared that the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم would divide the spoils (الغنائم) and the belongings of the slain (اسلاب القتلى) among those who fought, and would not give anything to those who remained behind guarding the tent of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. So they differed among themselves until they asked the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, saying: "To whom do these spoils belong?" So Allah revealed: يَسْئَلُونَكَ عَنِ الْأَنْفالِ قُلِ الْأَنْفالُ لِلَّهِ وَالرَّسُولِ ** (They ask you concerning the spoils. Say, 'The spoils belong to Allah and the Apostle.') So the people returned, having nothing of the spoils. Then, after that, Allah revealed: وَاعْلَمُوا أَنَّما غَنِمْتُمْ ** (the Ayah). So the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم divided it among them. Then Sa'd ibn Abi Waqqas said: "O Messenger of Allah, do you give the horseman (فارس) of the people, who protects them, the same as you give to the weak one (الضعيف)?" The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "May your mother mourn you! Are you helped except through your weak ones?" He said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم did not take the fifth (يخمّس) at Badr, but divided it among his companions. Then He began taking the fifth (الخُمس) after Badr. فَاتَّقُوا اللهَ ** (So be wary of Allah) regarding the disagreement (الإِختلاف) and quarreling (المشاجرة), وَأَصْلِحُوا ذاتَ بَيْنِكُمْ ** (and set right your mutual relations)—the state (الحال) between you—through consolation (المواساة) and assistance (المساعدة) in what Allah has provided you, and by submitting the matter to Allah and the Messenger, وَأَطِيعُوا اللهَ وَرَسُولَهُ ** (and obey Allah and His Apostle) concerning it, إِنْ كُنْتُمْ مُؤْمِنِينَ ** (if you are faithful), for faith (الإِيمان) necessitates that.

(٢) إِنَّمَا الْمُؤْمِنُونَ ** (The faithful are only) meaning, those who are perfect (الكاملون) in faith (الإِيمان), الَّذِينَ إِذا ذُكِرَ اللهُ وَجِلَتْ قُلُوبُهُمْ ** (those whose hearts tremble when Allah is mentioned)—their hearts are terrified (فزعت) at His mention, out of reverence (استعظاماً) for Him and awe (هيبة) of His majesty (جلاله), وَإِذا تُلِيَتْ عَلَيْهِمْ آياتُهُ زادَتْهُمْ إِيماناً ** (and when His signs are recited to them, they increase their faith)—they increase in certainty (يقيناً) and tranquility of soul (طمأنينة نفس) through them, وَعَلى رَبِّهِمْ يَتَوَكَّلُونَ ** (and in their Lord do they put their trust)—and to Him do they entrust (يفوّضون) their affairs concerning what they fear and what they hope for.

(٣) الَّذِينَ يُقِيمُونَ الصَّلاةَ وَمِمَّا رَزَقْناهُمْ يُنْفِقُونَ. (3) those who maintain the prayer and spend out of what We have provided them. (٤) أُولئِكَ هُمُ الْمُؤْمِنُونَ حَقًّا ** (4) It is they who are the true faithful. because they actualized their faith by joining to it noble morals (مكارم الأخلاق) and the good deeds of the limbs (أفعال الجوارح). لَهُمْ دَرَجاتٌ عِنْدَ رَبِّهِمْ ** They shall have ranks near their Lord, as an honor (كرامة) and elevation of status (علوّ منزلة), and forgiveness for what they neglected (فرط منهم). وَرِزْقٌ كَرِيمٌ ** and forgiveness and a noble provision. prepared for them in Paradise (الجنّة). القمّيّ** (narrated): It was revealed concerning the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), Abu Dharr, Salman, and Miqdad.

In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): By the completeness (تمام) of faith, the believers entered Paradise (الجنّة). And by the increase (الزيادة) in faith, the believers surpassed one another in ranks (بالدّرجات) with Allah. And by the deficiency (النقصان), the negligent (المفرّطون) entered the Fire (النار). The beginning (صدر) of this narration will come in the final parts of Surat al-Tawbah, if Allah wills.

(5) كَما أَخْرَجَكَ رَبُّكَ مِنْ بَيْتِكَ بِالْحَقِّ وَإِنَّ فَرِيقاً مِنَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ لَكارِهُونَ ** (5) just as your Lord brought you out of your house with the truth, while a part of the faithful were surely averse. It was said: meaning, this state of theirs in being averse to what Allah decreed concerning the spoils (الأنفال) is like their state in being averse to your departure from your house for the war. And in al-Majma', in the hadith of Abu Hamzah: "So Allah is your Helper, كَما أَخْرَجَكَ مِنْ بَيْتِكَ.**" (just as He brought you out of your house.)

(٦) يُجادِلُونَكَ فِي الْحَقِ ** (They dispute with you concerning the truth) regarding your preference for al-Jihad (الجهاد) to manifest the truth, rather than their preference for intercepting the caravan and seizing the abundant wealth, over meeting the mobilization (النفير) and fighting (الجهاد) alongside the large multitude (الجمّ الغفير), even after it had become clear that they would be granted victory wherever they turned with the banners of the Messenger (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم). كَأَنَّما يُساقُونَ إِلَى الْمَوْتِ وَهُمْ يَنْظُرُونَ ** (as if they were being driven to death while they watched.) That is, they disliked fighting with the dislike of one who is driven to death while he witnesses its causes (أسبابه). This was due to the smallness of their number and their lack of preparedness for fighting (القتال).

(٧) وَإِذْ يَعِدُكُمُ اللهُ ** (7) And when Allah promised you one of the two parties that it would be yours [is understood] on the suppressed [verb] 'remember' (اذكر). إِحْدَى الطَّائِفَتَيْنِ أَنَّها لَكُمْ ** meaning the caravan (العير) or the armed force (النفير). وَتَوَدُّونَ أَنَّ غَيْرَ ذاتِ الشَّوْكَةِ ** (1) [meaning] the intensity (الحدّة). تَكُونُ لَكُمْ ** meaning the caravan (العير), for there were only forty horsemen in it, and for that reason, they wished for it and disliked meeting the armed force (النَّفِير) due to the large number of their men and their equipment (2).

Al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): **ذاتِ الشَّوْكَةِ ** is the one in which there is fighting.

يُرِيدُ اللهُ أَنْ يُحِقَّ الْحَقَ ** Allah desires to uphold the truth with His words and to sever the root of the faithless that He establish it and elevate it with His words (بِكَلِماتِهِ). It was said: [meaning] with His verses (بآياتِهِ) revealed concerning fighting them, or with His allies (بأوليائه).

And Al-Qummi said that the words (الكلمات) are the Imams (peace be upon them). وَيَقْطَعَ دابِرَ الْكافِرِينَ ** and eradicate them (ويستأصلهم). And the meaning is that you desire wealth without encountering any hardship, while Allah desires the elevation of the religion and the manifestation of the truth, and that through which you achieve the triumph of both abodes.

** (٨) **لِيُحِقَّ الْحَقَّ وَيُبْطِلَ الْباطِلَ ** (8) that He may uphold the truth and refute the falsehood.

He did what He did, and it is not a repetition, because the first [mention] is for clarifying the intent of Allah and the difference between it and their intent, and the second is for clarifying the motive that led the Messenger to choose the one possessing strength (ذات الشّوكة) and His granting him victory over it, **وَلَوْ كَرِهَ الْمُجْرِمُونَ ** that.

footnote (١) *الشّوكة* (strength/might) is the severity of might (*شدّة البأس*) and sharpness (*الحدّة*) in weaponry. It is said: *شاك الرّجل* (the man became mighty) from the category (*باب*) of *خاف* (to fear), meaning his might (*شوكته*) and sharpness (*حدّته*) appeared. Thus, he is *شائك السلاح* (possessing sharp weaponry) or *شاكي السّلاح* (possessing sharp weaponry) by inversion (*على القلب*).

footnote (٢) It is conjoined (*عطف*) to *كثرة* (abundance) and not to *عددهم* (their number), meaning: due to the abundance of their number and due to their preparation (*تأهبهم*) and readiness (*استعدادهم*).

(٩) إِذْ تَسْتَغِيثُونَ رَبَّكُمْ ** (When you sought the aid of your Lord) When you knew that there was no escape (محيص) from fighting, despite your small number and the multitude of your enemy.

It is a substitute (بدل) for إِذْ يَعِدُكُمُ. (when He promised you).

In al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): When the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم looked at the great number of the polytheists (المشركين) and the small number of the Muslims, he faced the Qiblah and said: "O Allah, fulfill for me what You promised me. O Allah, if this small band (العصابة) perishes, You will not be worshipped on earth." He continued to call upon his Lord, stretching out his hands, until his cloak (رداؤه) fell from his shoulder. Then Allah revealed إِذْ تَسْتَغِيثُونَ ** the Ayah.

فَاسْتَجابَ لَكُمْ أَنِّي مُمِدُّكُمْ بِأَلْفٍ مِنَ الْمَلائِكَةِ مُرْدِفِينَ ** (So He responded to you: 'I will reinforce you with a thousand angels, following one another.') Following the believers, or some of them following others. It is derived from (أردفته) (I made him follow) when I came after him.

And it was read with the opening (فتح) of the dāl (الدّال) [i.e., مُرْدَفِينَ], and this is derived from (أردفته إيّاه) (I made him follow him).

(١٠) وَما جَعَلَهُ اللهُ إِلَّا بُشْرى وَلِتَطْمَئِنَّ بِهِ قُلُوبُكُمْ وَمَا النَّصْرُ إِلَّا مِنْ عِنْدِ اللهِ إِنَّ اللهَ عَزِيزٌ حَكِيمٌ ** (10) And Allah did not appoint it except as a good news and that your hearts may be at rest thereby, and victory comes only from Allah. Indeed Allah is all-mighty, all-wise.**

The Almighty's saying: وَما جَعَلَهُ اللهُ ** meaning the reinforcement (الإِمداد) إِلَّا بُشْرى ** a glad tiding for you of victory وَلِتَطْمَئِنَّ بِهِ قُلُوبُكُمْ ** so that the fear (الوَجَل) within them may vanish, due to your small numbers and your weakness (ذلّتِكم). وَمَا النَّصْرُ إِلَّا مِنْ عِنْدِ اللهِ إِنَّ اللهَ عَزِيزٌ حَكِيمٌ ** And the reinforcements of the angels (أمداد الملائكة) and the large number (كثرة العدد) are merely means (وسائط) that have no effect (تأثير) in themselves. Therefore, do not reckon victory to be from them, nor despair of it upon their absence.

(١١) إِذْ يُغَشِّيكُمُ النُّعاسَ أَمَنَةً مِنْهُ ** (11) When He covered you with a slumber, as a security from Him, a security (أمناً) from Allah, a second appositive (بدل ثان) for إِذْ يَعِدُكُمُ ** (when He promised you), to show a third blessing. The meaning is: when you fell asleep due to the security granted to you by Allah, by removing the terror from your hearts. وَيُنَزِّلُ عَلَيْكُمْ مِنَ السَّماءِ ماءً لِيُطَهِّرَكُمْ بِهِ ** (and sent down water upon you from the sky, that He might purify you with it) from ritual impurity (الحَدَث) and filth (الخبث).

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "Drink the water of the sky, for it purifies the body and repels illnesses." Then He (peace be upon him) recited this verse.

A similar narration is found in al-Khisal and al-Ayyashi from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): وَيُذْهِبَ عَنْكُمْ رِجْزَ الشَّيْطانِ ** (and remove from you the defilement of Satan) means major ritual impurity (الجنابة). This is because some of them had nocturnal emissions (احتلم) and the polytheists had control over the water.

It is also possible that the intent of the defilement (رجز) of Satan is his whispering (وسوسته) and his frightening them of thirst, since it is narrated that they had encamped on a barren sand dune (كثيب اعقر) where feet would sink (تسوخ فيه الأقدام), and there was no water. They slept, and most of them had nocturnal emissions (احتلم). The polytheists had gained control over the water. So, Satan whispered to them and said: "How can you be victorious when you have been overcome regarding the water, and you are praying while ritually impure (محدثين) and in a state of major ritual impurity (مجنبين), yet you claim that you are the friends (أولياء) of Allah and His Messenger is among you?" So, they became distressed (أشفقوا). Then Allah sent down the rain, and they were rained upon at night until the valley flowed. They made cisterns (الحياض) on its bank (عدوته) (1), watered the riding beasts (الركاب) (2), performed the major ablution (اغتسلوا), performed the minor ablution (توضئوا), and the sand between them and the enemy became compacted (تلبّد) (3), so that feet became firm upon it and the whispering (الوسوسة) ceased. وَلِيَرْبِطَ عَلى قُلُوبِكُمْ ** (and strengthen your hearts) by relying upon the kindness (لطف) of Allah, the Exalted, towards you, وَيُثَبِّتَ بِهِ الْأَقْدامَ ** (and make your feet steady with it) by means of the rain, so that they would not sink (تسوخ) in the sand, or by means of strengthening (الرّبطِ) the hearts, so that they would remain firm in the battle.

(١٢) إِذْ يُوحِي رَبُّكَ A third badal (بدل) to manifest a fourth blessing إِلَى الْمَلائِكَةِ أَنِّي مَعَكُمْ in assisting them and strengthening them فَثَبِّتُوا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا by giving them glad tidings, by increasing their ranks (سوادهم), and by fighting their enemies سَأُلْقِي فِي قُلُوبِ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا الرُّعْبَ فَاضْرِبُوا فَوْقَ الْأَعْناقِ (I will cast terror into the hearts of the faithless. So strike above the necks) their upper parts, which are the place of slaughter (المذبح) and the heads وَاضْرِبُوا مِنْهُمْ كُلَّ بَنانٍ ** (and strike every fingertip of them.) (meaning) fingers (أصابع), that is, sever their necks and cut off their extremities (أطرافهم).

(13) ذلِكَ بِأَنَّهُمْ شَاقُّوا اللهَ وَرَسُولَهُ (13) That is because they defied Allah and His Apostle, due to their defiance of the two of them, and their being on a side (شِقّ) contrary to their side (شِقّهما). وَمَنْ يُشاقِقِ اللهَ وَرَسُولَهُ فَإِنَّ اللهَ شَدِيدُ الْعِقابِ. And whoever defies Allah and His Apostle, Allah is indeed severe in retribution. (14) ذلِكُمْ (14) That is for you. The address therein is directed at the disbelievers, following the method of iltifāt (الالتفات) (shift in address). فَذُوقُوهُ وَأَنَّ لِلْكافِرِينَ عَذابَ النَّارِ So taste it, and [know] that there is the punishment of the Fire for the faithless. And the meaning is: Taste what has been hastened for you of killing and captivity, along with what has been deferred for you in the Hereafter of the punishment of the Fire.

Al-Qummiyy (القمّيّ) [said]: The reason for this was that the caravan (عير) of Quraysh, containing their treasures, had departed for Syria (الشام). The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم commanded his companions to go out to seize it, informing them that Allah, the Exalted, had promised him one of the two parties (الطائفتين): either the caravan or Quraysh, if He granted him victory over them. So, he departed with three hundred and thirteen men. When he approached Badr, Abu Sufyan (may Allah curse him) was with the caravan. When he learned that the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم had departed to intercept the caravan, he became intensely afraid and proceeded toward Syria. When he reached al-Nuqrah (النقرة) (1), he hired Ḍamḍam ibn ʿAmr al-Khuzāʿī (ضمضم بن عمرو الخزاعيّ) for ten dinars (دنانير) and gave him a young she-camel (قلوص) (2). He told him: "Go to Quraysh and inform them that Muḥammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and the apostates (الصباة) (3) from the people of Yathrib have departed to intercept your caravan, so catch up with the caravan." He instructed him to saddle his she-camel, cut its ear until blood flowed, and tear his garment from front and back. When he entered Mecca, he was to turn his face toward the tail of the caravan and shout at the top of his voice, saying: "O family of Ghālib! O family of Ghālib! The precious goods (اللطيمة) (4)! The precious goods! The caravan! The caravan! Catch up! Catch up! I do not think you will catch up, for Muḥammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and the apostates (الصباة) from the people of Yathrib have departed to intercept your caravan."

So Ḍamḍam departed, rushing toward Mecca. Three days before Ḍamḍam’s arrival, ʿĀtikah bint ʿAbd al-Muṭṭalib (عاتكة بنت عبد المطلب) saw in her sleep as if a rider had entered Mecca, calling out: "O family of treachery! O family of treachery! Go forth to your slaughtering places on the morning of the third day!" Then he arrived with his camel at Abū Qubays (أبي قبيس), took a stone, and rolled it down the mountain. There was no house among the houses of Quraysh that was not struck by a fragment of it, and it was as if the valley of Mecca flowed with blood from its lower part.

She awoke terrified and informed al-ʿAbbās (العباس) of this. Al-ʿAbbās informed ʿUtbah ibn Rabīʿah (عتبة بن ربيعة). ʿUtbah said: "This is a calamity that will befall Quraysh." The dream spread among Quraysh, and this reached Abū Jahl (أبا جهل). He said: "ʿĀtikah did not see this dream; this is a second fabrication (تبنية) among the Banū ʿAbd al-Muṭṭalib (بني عبد المطلب). By al-Lāt and al-ʿUzzā (واللّات والعزّى), we shall wait three days. If what she saw is true, then it is as she saw. But if it is otherwise, we shall write a document among ourselves that there is no household among the Arabs whose men and women are greater liars than the Banū Hāshim (بني هاشم)." When one day passed, Abū Jahl said: "This day has passed." When the second day came, Abū Jahl said: "These two days have passed." When the third day came, Ḍamḍam arrived, shouting in the valley: "O family of Ghālib! O family of Ghālib! The precious goods (اللطيمة)! The precious goods! The caravan! The caravan! Catch up! Catch up! I do not think you will catch up, for Muḥammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and the apostates (الصّباة) from the people of Yathrib have departed to intercept your caravan, which contains your treasures."

The people in Mecca shouted and prepared to depart. Suhayl ibn ʿAmr (سهل بن عمرو), Ṣafwān ibn Umayyah (صفوان بن أميّة), Abū al-Bakhtarī ibn Hishām (أبو البختري بن هشام), Munabbih (1) and Nubayh (منبّه ونبيه) the sons of al-Ḥajjāj (ابنا الحجّاج), and Nawfal ibn Khuwaylid (نوفل بن خويلد) stood up and said: "O assembly of Quraysh! By Allah, no calamity greater than this has afflicted you: that Muḥammad and the apostates (الصّباة) from the people of Yathrib should aspire to intercept your caravan which contains your treasures! By Allah, there is no man or woman of Quraysh who does not have twenty dirhams (نَشّ) (2) or more in this caravan. It is indeed humiliation and disgrace that Muḥammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم should covet your wealth and separate you from your trade route, so go forth!"

Ṣafwān ibn Umayyah contributed five hundred dinars (دنانير) and equipped [men] with it. Suhayl ibn ʿAmr contributed, and there remained none of the great men of Quraysh who did not contribute wealth, load [provisions], and strengthen [the army]. They departed upon the difficult (الصعب) (3) and the docile [mounts], unable to control themselves, just as Allah, the Exalted, said: خَرَجُوا مِنْ دِيارِهِمْ بَطَراً وَرِئاءَ النَّاسِ who left their homes boastfully and to be seen by people, Al-ʿAbbās ibn ʿAbd al-Muṭṭalib (العباس بن عبد المطلب), Nawfal ibn al-Ḥārith (نوفل بن الحرث), and ʿAqīl ibn Abī Ṭālib (عقيل بن أبي طالب) departed with them. They also brought along singing slave-girls (القِيان) (4), drinking wine and beating tambourines (الدّفوف).

The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم departed with three hundred and thirteen men. When he was near Badr, about a night's journey away, he sent Bashīr ibn Abī al-Raghbāʾ (بشير بن أبي الرّغباء) and Muḥammad ibn ʿAmr (محمّد بن عمرو) to scout for news of the caravan. They came to the water of Badr, made their riding beasts kneel, and drank the sweet water. They heard two slave-girls clinging to each other, one demanding a dirham (درهم) that the other owed her. She said: "The caravan of Quraysh camped yesterday in such-and-such a place, and tomorrow it will camp here. I will work for them and pay you back." They returned and informed him of what they had heard.

Then Abū Sufyān approached with the caravan. When he neared Badr, he went ahead of the caravan and came alone until he reached the water of Badr. There was a man from Juhaynah (جهينة) there named Kisb al-Juhanī (كِسب الجهني). He said to him: "O Kisb, do you have any knowledge of Muḥammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and his companions?" He replied: "No." [Abū Sufyān] said: "By al-Lāt and al-ʿUzzā, if you conceal the matter of Muḥammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم from us, Quraysh will remain hostile to you forever, for there is no one from Quraysh who does not have twenty dirhams (نَشّ) or more in this caravan, so do not conceal [anything] from me."

He replied: "By Allah, I have no knowledge of Muḥammad and his companions through direct information, except that today I saw two riders who arrived, drank the sweet water, made their riding beasts kneel, and then departed. I do not know who they were." Abū Sufyān went to the place where their camels had knelt and broke open the camels' dung with his hand. He found date stones (النّوى) in it and said: "These are the fodder of Yathrib! By Allah, these are Muḥammad's spies (عيون)." He returned quickly and ordered the caravan to be taken toward the seashore. They abandoned the road and passed quickly.

Gabriel (جبرئيل) descended upon the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and informed him that the caravan had escaped and that Quraysh had arrived to defend their caravan. He commanded him to fight and promised him victory. He was camped at the water of al-Ṣafrāʾ (ماء الصّفراءِ). He wished to test the Anṣār (الأنصار) because they had only promised to support Him while He was in the city (في الدّار). So, he informed them that the caravan had passed and that Quraysh had arrived to defend their caravan, and that Allah had commanded him to fight them.

The companions of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم were distressed by this and feared greatly. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "Advise me." Abū Bakr (أبو بكر) stood up and said: "O Messenger of Allah, this is Quraysh and its arrogance. It has not believed since it disbelieved, nor has it been humbled since it gained might. We did not depart prepared for war." The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "Sit down," so he sat. He then said: "Advise me." ʿUmar (عمر) stood up and said the same as Abū Bakr. He said: "Sit down."

Then al-Miqdād (المقداد) stood up and said: "O Messenger of Allah! This is Quraysh and its arrogance, but we have believed in you, affirmed you, and testified that what you have brought is the truth from Allah. Even if you commanded us to wade through the embers of al-Ghaḍā (الغضا) (1) and the thorns of al-Hirās (الهراس), we would wade through them with you. We will not say to you what the Children of Israel said to Moses: فَاذْهَبْ أَنْتَ وَرَبُّكَ فَقاتِلا إِنَّا هاهُنا قاعِدُونَ So go ahead, you and your Lord, and fight. We will stay right here. Rather, we say: 'Go ahead, you and your Lord, and fight. We are fighting alongside you.'" The Prophet rewarded him well, and then he sat down.

Then he said: "Advise me." Saʿd ibn Muʿādh (سعد بن معاذ) stood up and said: "May my father and mother be ransomed for you, O Messenger of Allah! It seems you meant us?" He said: "Yes." He said: "Perhaps you departed for a matter other than what you were commanded?" He said: "Yes." He said: "May my father and mother be ransomed for you, O Messenger of Allah! We have believed in you, affirmed you, and testified that what you have brought is the truth from Allah. So command us whatever you wish, and take from our wealth whatever you wish, and leave whatever you wish. What you take is dearer to me than what you leave. By Allah, if you commanded us to wade through this sea, we would wade through it with you." Then he said: "May my father and mother be ransomed for you, O Messenger of Allah! By Allah, I have never traveled this road, and I have no knowledge of it. We have left behind people in Medina who are no less zealous in fighting for you than we are. If they had known it was war, they would not have stayed behind. But we will prepare the riding beasts for you and meet our enemy, for we are steadfast (صُبَّرٌ) in encounter and brave (أنجاد) in war. We hope that Allah will gladden your eyes through us. If what you desire happens, then so be it. If it is otherwise, you may mount your riding beasts and rejoin our people."

The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "Allah will bring about something other than that. It is as if I see the place of death (مصرع) of so-and-so here, and the place of death of so-and-so here, and the place of death of Abū Jahl, ʿUtbah ibn Rabīʿah, Shaybah ibn Rabīʿah (شيبة بن ربيعة), and Munabbih and Nubayh, the sons of al-Ḥajjāj. For Allah has promised me one of the two parties, and Allah does not break His promise." Then Gabriel descended upon the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم with this verse: كَما أَخْرَجَكَ رَبُّكَ مِنْ بَيْتِكَ بِالْحَقِ Just as your Lord brought you out of your home with the truth, up to His saying: وَلَوْ كَرِهَ الْمُجْرِمُونَ though the guilty are averse. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم commanded the departure until they camped in the evening at the water of Badr, which was the Syrian side (العدوة الشّامية). Quraysh approached and camped on the Yemeni side (العدوة اليمانيّة). They sent their slaves to fetch sweet water. The companions of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم seized them and detained them. They asked them: "Who are you?" They replied: "We are slaves of Quraysh." They asked: "Where is the caravan?" They replied: "We have no knowledge of the caravan." So, they began to beat them. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم was praying (يصلِّي). He turned away from his prayer and said: "If they speak the truth to you, you beat them, and if they lie to you, you leave them! Bring them to me." So, they brought them.

He asked them: "Who are you?" They replied: "O Muḥammad, we are slaves of Quraysh." He asked: "How many are the people?" They replied: "We do not know their number." He asked: "How many camels (جزوراً) (1) do they slaughter every day?" They replied: "Nine to ten." The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "The people are nine hundred to a thousand." He asked: "Who among the Banū Hāshim (بني هاشم) are with them?" They replied: "Al-ʿAbbās ibn ʿAbd al-Muṭṭalib, Nawfal ibn al-Ḥārith, and ʿAqīl ibn Abī Ṭālib." The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم commanded that they be detained.

This reached Quraysh, and they feared greatly. ʿUtbah ibn Rabīʿah met Abū al-Bakhtarī ibn Hishām (أبا البختري بن هشام) and said to him: "Do you not see this transgression (البغي)? By Allah, I cannot see the place of my own feet! We departed to defend our caravan, and it has escaped. We have come out in transgression and hostility (بغياً وعدواناً). By Allah, no people who transgressed have ever succeeded. I wish that all the wealth of the Banū ʿAbd Manāf (بني عبد مناف) in the caravan had been lost, and we had not undertaken this journey."

Abū al-Bakhtarī said to him: "You are a master among the masters of Quraysh. Go among the people and take responsibility for the caravan that Muḥammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم seized at Nakhla (نخلة), and the blood (دَم) (2) of Ibn al-Ḥaḍramī (ابن الحضرمي), for he is your ally." ʿUtbah said: "You advise me to do that? No one among us will oppose [this] except the son of the Ḥanẓaliyyah (meaning Abū Jahl). Go to him and inform him that I have taken responsibility for the caravan that Muḥammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم seized at Nakhla, and the blood of Ibn al-Ḥaḍramī."

Abū al-Bakhtarī said: "So I went to his tent, and behold, he had taken out his coat of mail (درع). I said to him: 'Abū al-Walīd (أبو الوليد) sent me to you with a message.' He became angry and then said: 'Did ʿUtbah not find a messenger other than you?' I replied: 'By Allah, if anyone else had sent me, I would not have come, but Abū al-Walīd is the master of the clan.' He became angry again and said: 'You say he is the master of the clan?' I replied: 'I say it, and all of Quraysh says that he has taken responsibility for the caravan and the blood of Ibn al-Ḥaḍramī.' He said: 'ʿUtbah is the most eloquent of people and the most articulate in speech. He is biased toward Muḥammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم because he is from the Banū ʿAbd Manāf, and his son is with him, and he wants not to let him down among the people. No, by al-Lāt and al-ʿUzzā, [we will not return] until we plunge (نقحم) (1) upon them in Yathrib and take them captive, bringing them into Mecca, so that the Arabs may hear of this, and there will be no one we dislike between us and our trade route.'"

The companions of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم learned of the large number of Quraysh, so they were severely frightened, complained, wept, and sought help. Then Allah revealed to His Messenger صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: إِذْ تَسْتَغِيثُونَ رَبَّكُمْ فَاسْتَجابَ لَكُمْ أَنِّي مُمِدُّكُمْ بِأَلْفٍ مِنَ الْمَلائِكَةِ مُرْدِفِينَ وَما جَعَلَهُ اللهُ إِلَّا بُشْرى وَلِتَطْمَئِنَّ بِهِ قُلُوبُكُمْ وَمَا النَّصْرُ إِلَّا مِنْ عِنْدِ اللهِ إِنَّ اللهَ عَزِيزٌ حَكِيمٌ When you sought the help of your Lord, He answered you: ‘I will reinforce you with a thousand angels in a continuous stream.’ Allah did not appoint it but as a good news, and so that your hearts might be at rest thereby; and victory comes only from Allah. Indeed, Allah is all-mighty, all-wise. When evening came for the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and night covered them (2), Allah, the Exalted, cast drowsiness (النّعاس) upon His companions until they slept, and Allah, the Exalted, sent down rain (السماء) (3) upon them. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم had camped in a place where the foot could not stand firm, so Allah sent down rain upon them and compacted (لبد) the earth so that their feet would be firm. This is the meaning of Allah, the Exalted's, saying: إِذْ يُغَشِّيكُمُ النُّعاسَ أَمَنَةً مِنْهُ وَيُنَزِّلُ عَلَيْكُمْ مِنَ السَّماءِ ماءً لِيُطَهِّرَكُمْ بِهِ وَيُذْهِبَ عَنْكُمْ رِجْزَ الشَّيْطانِ When He covered you with drowsiness as a security from Him, and He sent down water upon you from the sky, that He might purify you with it, and remove from you the defilement of Satan—and that was because some of the Prophet’s companions صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم had experienced a wet dream (احتلم)— وَلِيَرْبِطَ عَلى قُلُوبِكُمْ وَيُثَبِّتَ بِهِ الْأَقْدامَ and that He might fortify your hearts and make your feet steady with it. The rain upon Quraysh was like water pouring from waterskins (العزالي) (4), while upon the companions of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم it was a light drizzle (رَذاذاً), just enough to compact the earth. Quraysh feared greatly, so they began to guard themselves, fearing a night attack (البيات). The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم sent ʿAmmār ibn Yāsir (عمّار بن ياسر) and ʿAbd Allāh ibn Masʿūd (عبد الله بن مسعود) and said: "Enter among the people and bring us their news." They moved around their camp, seeing only fearful, terrified people. If a horse neighed, [the rider] would jump onto its lip (جحفلته) (5). They heard Munabbih ibn al-Ḥajjāj saying:

Hunger will not let us sleep the night.

We must either die or He will kill us.

[The narrator] said: "By Allah, they were full, but they said this out of fear." And Allah cast terror (الرّعب) into their hearts, just as Allah, the Exalted, said: سَأُلْقِي فِي قُلُوبِ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا الرُّعْبَ I will cast terror into the hearts of the faithless. When morning came, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم arranged (عبّا) (1) his companions. In the army of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم there were two horses: a horse belonging to al-Zubayr ibn al-ʿAwwām (الزّبير بن العوام) and a horse belonging to al-Miqdād (مقداد). In his army, there were seventy camels upon which they rode in turns. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, ʿAlī ibn Abī Ṭālib (peace be upon him), and Marthad ibn Abī Marthad al-Ghanawī (مرثد بن أبي مرثد الغنويّ) rode a camel in turns, and the camel belonged to Marthad. In the army of Quraysh, there were four hundred horses. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم arranged his companions before him and said: "Lower your gazes, do not initiate the fighting, and let no one speak." When Quraysh saw the small number of the companions of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم,

Abū Jahl said: "They are nothing but a mouthful (أكلة رأس). If we sent our slaves to them, they would seize them by hand." ʿUtbah ibn Rabīʿah said: "Do you think they have an ambush (كميناً) or reinforcements (مدداً)?" So, they sent ʿAmr ibn Wahb al-Jumaḥī (عمرو بن وهب الجُمَحي), who was a brave knight. He rode his horse until he circled the army of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, then he ascended the valley and shouted, then returned to Quraysh and said: "They have neither an ambush nor reinforcements. However, the water-carrying camels (نواضح) (2) of Yathrib have brought deadly poison (الموت الناقع). Do you not see them silent, not speaking, licking their lips like vipers? They have no refuge except their swords. I do not think they will turn back until they are killed, nor will they be killed until you are killed in their number. So, consider (فارتئوا) (3) your opinion." Abū Jahl said: "You lied and were cowardly, and your lung (سحْرُك) swelled up," meaning: "You looked at the swords of the people of Yathrib."

The companions of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم were terrified when they saw the large number and strength of Quraysh. Then Allah, the Exalted, revealed to His Messenger: وَإِنْ جَنَحُوا لِلسَّلْمِ فَاجْنَحْ لَها وَتَوَكَّلْ عَلَى اللهِ And if they incline to peace, incline to it too, and put your trust in Allah. Allah already knew that they would not incline or respond to peace. Allah, the Exalted, only intended by this to reassure the hearts of the Prophet’s companions صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم.

The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم sent [a message] to Quraysh, saying: "O assembly of Quraysh! I find no one among the Arabs whom I would hate more to initiate [fighting] with than you. So, leave me and the Arabs. If I am truthful, you will be the most observant of me. If I am a liar, the wolves (ذؤبان) of the Arabs will suffice you in my matter. So, return."

ʿUtbah said: "By Allah, no people who rejected this have ever succeeded." Then he mounted his red camel. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم looked at him riding around the army, forbidding fighting, and said: "If there is any good with anyone, it is with the owner of the red camel. If you obey him, you will be rightly guided." ʿUtbah approached, saying: "O assembly of Quraysh, gather and listen!" Then he addressed them, saying: "A blessing (يُمْن) with spaciousness (رحْب) (1), and spaciousness with a blessing! O assembly of Quraysh! Obey me today and disobey me forever! Return to Mecca, drink wine, and embrace the beautiful women (الحور). Muḥammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم has a covenant (إلّ) (2) and a guarantee (ذمّة), and he is your cousin. So, return, and do not reject my opinion. You are only demanding from Muḥammad the caravan that Muḥammad seized at Nakhla, and the blood of Ibn al-Ḥaḍramī, who is my ally, and his blood-money (عقله) is upon me."

When Abū Jahl heard that, it enraged him. He said: "ʿUtbah is the most eloquent of people and the most articulate in speech. If Quraysh returns based on his word, he will be the master of Quraysh forever." Then he said: "O ʿUtbah, you looked at the swords of the Banū ʿAbd al-Muṭṭalib, became cowardly, and your lung (سَحْرُك) (3) swelled up! You command the people to return when we have seen our tracks with our own eyes!" ʿUtbah dismounted his camel and charged at Abū Jahl, who was on a horse. He grabbed him by his hair. The people said: "He will kill him!" So, he hamstrung his horse and said: "Should someone like me be called cowardly? Quraysh will know today which of us is the baser and the more cowardly, and which of us is the corruptor of his people! No one walks toward manifest death except me and you." Then he said: "This is my harvest, and the best of it is in it, and every harvester puts his hand to his mouth." Then he grabbed him by his hair, dragging him. The people gathered around him and said: "O Abū al-Walīd! Fear Allah! Do not weaken the resolve of the people! You forbid something, yet you are the first to do it!" So, they rescued Abū Jahl from his hand.

ʿUtbah looked at his brother Shaybah and looked at his son al-Walīd (الوليد). He said: "Stand up, my son," so he stood. Then he put on his coat of mail. They sought a helmet (بيضة) large enough for his head but could not find one due to the size of his skull (هامته). So, he wrapped two turbans around his head. Then he took his sword and advanced, along with his brother and his son, and called out: "O Muḥammad, send out our equals (أكفّاءنا) from Quraysh to us!" Three men from the Anṣār came forward: ʿAwf (عوذ), Muʿawwidh (معوذ), and ʿAwn (عون), the sons of ʿAfrāʾ (بني عفراء). ʿUtbah said: "Who are you? State your lineage so we may know you." They replied: "We are the sons of ʿAfrāʾ, the helpers (أنصار) of Allah and the helpers of His Messenger." He said: "Go back, for we do not seek you. We only seek our equals from Quraysh." The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم sent word to them to return, so they returned, as he disliked that the first charge (الكرّة) should be by the Anṣār. They returned and stood in their position.

Then the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم looked at ʿUbaydah ibn al-Ḥārith ibn ʿAbd al-Muṭṭalib (عبيدة بن الحرث بن عبد المطلب), who was seventy years old, and said to him: "Stand up, O ʿUbaydah." So, he stood before Him with his sword. Then he looked at Ḥamzah ibn ʿAbd al-Muṭṭalib (حمزة بن عبد المطلب) and said to him: "Stand up, O uncle." Then he looked at the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) and said to him: "Stand up, O ʿAlī." He was the youngest of the people in age. They stood before the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم with their swords: "Seek your right which Allah has established for you. Quraysh has come with its arrogance and pride, seeking to extinguish the light of Allah. وَيَأْبَى اللهُ إِلَّا أَنْ يُتِمَّ نُورَهُ. But Allah is intent on perfecting His light, though the faithless should be averse." Then the Messenger of Allah said: "O ʿUbaydah, take on ʿUtbah." He said to Ḥamzah: "Take on Shaybah." And he said to ʿAlī: "Take on al-Walīd ibn ʿUtbah." They proceeded until they reached the people. ʿUtbah said: "Who are you? State your lineage so we may know you." He replied: "I am ʿUbaydah ibn al-Ḥārith ibn ʿAbd al-Muṭṭalib." He said: "A noble equal (كفو كريم)." He asked: "And who are these two?" He replied: "Ḥamzah ibn ʿAbd al-Muṭṭalib and ʿAlī ibn Abī Ṭālib." He said: "Two noble equals. May Allah curse whoever brought us and you to this stand." Shaybah said to Ḥamzah: "Who are you?" He replied: "I am Ḥamzah ibn ʿAbd al-Muṭṭalib, the Lion of Allah and the Lion of His Messenger." Shaybah said to him: "You have been nicknamed the Lion of the Ḥulafāʾ (أسد الحلفاء) (1), so let us see how fierce your attack will be, O Lion of Allah."

ʿUbaydah charged ʿUtbah and struck him on his head with a blow that split his skull. ʿUtbah struck ʿUbaydah on his leg and severed it. Both fell down. Ḥamzah charged Shaybah, and they struck each other with their swords until they were blunted, each protecting himself with his shield (دَرقته). The Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) charged al-Walīd ibn ʿUtbah and struck him on the tendon of his shoulder, bringing the sword out from under his armpit. ʿAlī (peace be upon him) said: "He took his severed right hand with his left and struck my skull with it, and I thought the sky had fallen upon the earth."

Then Ḥamzah and Shaybah grappled with each other. The Muslims said: "O ʿAlī, do you not see that the dog has overpowered your uncle?" So, ʿAlī (peace be upon him) charged toward him and said: "O uncle, lower your head." Ḥamzah was taller than Shaybah, so Ḥamzah lowered his head into his chest. The Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) struck Shaybah on his head and split half of it off. Then he came to ʿUtbah, who still had a flicker of life, and finished him off. ʿUbaydah was carried between Ḥamzah and ʿAlī until they brought him to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم looked at him and wept. [ʿUbaydah] said: "O Messenger of Allah, may my father and mother be ransomed for you! Am I not a martyr (شهيد)?" He said: "Yes, you are the first martyr from my household (أهل بيتي)." He said: "If only your uncle were alive, he would know that I am more deserving of what he said than he was." He صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم asked: "Which of my uncles do you mean?" He replied: "Abū Ṭālib, where he says:

You lied, by the House of Allah, we will protect Muḥammad,

And we will not cease stabbing and fighting in his defense,

Nor will we surrender him until we are laid low around him,

And we forget our sons and wives."

The Messenger of Allah said: "Do you not see his son [ʿAlī] like the charging lion before Allah and His Messenger? And his other son [Jaʿfar] is fighting the enemies of Allah in the land of Abyssinia (أرض الحبشة)." [ʿUbaydah] said: "O Messenger of Allah, are You displeased with me in this state?" He replied: "I am not displeased with you, but I remembered my uncle, and I was saddened by that."

Abū Jahl said to Quraysh: "Do not rush or boast, as the two sons of Rabīʿah rushed and boasted! Attack the people of Yathrib and slaughter them completely (فاجزروهم جزراً). And seize the Quraysh [Muslims] and take them captive until we bring them into Mecca and make them realize the error (ضلالتهم) they were upon." A group of Quraysh had embraced Islam in Mecca, but their fathers detained them. They departed with Quraysh to Badr while they were in doubt (الشّكّ), suspicion (الارتياب), and hypocrisy (النفاق). Among them were Qays ibn al-Walīd ibn al-Mughīrah (قيس بن الوليد بن المغيرة), Abū Qays ibn al-Fākihah (أبو قيس بن الفاكهة), al-Ḥārith ibn Rabīʿah (الحرث بن ربيعة), ʿAlī ibn Umayyah ibn Khalaf (عليّ بن أميّة بن خلفّ), and al-ʿĀṣ ibn al-Munabbih (العاص بن المنبّه). When they saw the small number of Muḥammad’s companions صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, they said: "These poor people! Their religion has deceived them, and they will be killed immediately." Then Allah revealed to His Messenger: إِذْ يَقُولُ الْمُنافِقُونَ وَالَّذِينَ فِي قُلُوبِهِمْ مَرَضٌ غَرَّ هؤُلاءِ دِينُهُمْ وَمَنْ يَتَوَكَّلْ عَلَى اللهِ فَإِنَّ اللهَ عَزِيزٌ حَكِيمٌ. When the hypocrites and those in whose hearts was a sickness said, ‘Their religion has deluded these [Muslims].’ But whoever puts his trust in Allah, then Allah is indeed all-mighty, all-wise. Iblīs (may the curse be upon him) came to Quraysh in the form of Surāqah ibn Mālik (سراقة بن مالك) and said to them: "I am a protector (جار) for you. Hand over your banner to me." So, they handed it over to him. He came with his devils (شياطينه), terrifying the companions of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, making things appear illusory to them, and frightening them. Quraysh advanced, led by Iblīs carrying the banner. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم looked at him and said: "Lower your gazes, clench your back teeth (النواجد), and do not draw a sword until I give you permission." Then he raised his hands to the sky and said: "O Lord, if You destroy this small band (العصابة), You will not be worshipped. If You wish, You will not be worshipped, You will not be worshipped." Then he was overcome by a trance (الغشي). It lifted (1) from him, and he was wiping the sweat from his face, saying: "This is Gabriel, who has come to you with بِأَلْفٍ مِنَ الْمَلائِكَةِ مُرْدِفِينَ. a thousand angels in a continuous stream." [The narrator] said: "We looked, and behold, a black cloud with flashing lightning had settled upon the army of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, and a voice was saying: 'Advance, Ḥayzūm (حيزوم) (2)! Advance, Ḥayzūm!' We heard the clanking of weapons from the sky." Iblīs looked at Gabriel and retreated, throwing down the banner. Munabbih ibn al-Ḥajjāj grabbed the edges of his garment and said: "Woe to you, O Surāqah! You weaken (تفت) (3) the resolve of the people!" Iblīs kicked (فركله) (4) him in the chest and said: إِنِّي بَرِيءٌ مِنْكُمْ إِنِّي أَرى ما لا تَرَوْنَ إِنِّي أَخافُ اللهَ I am clear of you. I see what you do not see. I fear Allah. This is the saying of Allah: وَإِذْ زَيَّنَ لَهُمُ الشَّيْطانُ أَعْمالَهُمْ وَقالَ : لا غالِبَ لَكُمُ الْيَوْمَ مِنَ النَّاسِ وَإِنِّي جارٌ لَكُمْ فَلَمَّا تَراءَتِ الْفِئَتانِ نَكَصَ عَلى عَقِبَيْهِ وَقالَ إِنِّي بَرِيءٌ مِنْكُمْ إِنِّي أَرى ما لا تَرَوْنَ إِنِّي أَخافُ اللهَ وَاللهُ شَدِيدُ الْعِقابِ And when Satan made their deeds seem fair to them, and said, ‘No one can overcome you today from among mankind, and I am your protector.’ But when the two hosts sighted each other, he turned on his heels and said, ‘I am clear of you. I see what you do not see. I fear Allah.’ And Allah is severe in retribution. Then He, the Mighty and Majestic, said: وَلَوْ تَرى إِذْ يَتَوَفَّى الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا الْمَلائِكَةُ يَضْرِبُونَ وُجُوهَهُمْ وَأَدْبارَهُمْ وَذُوقُوا عَذابَ الْحَرِيقِ. If you could see when the angels take away the souls of the faithless, striking their faces and their backs, and [saying]: ‘Taste the punishment of the burning!’ Gabriel charged Iblīs and pursued him until he plunged into the sea, saying: "My Lord, fulfill for me what You promised me of remaining until the Day of Judgment." It is narrated in a report: Iblīs turned to Gabriel while fleeing and said: "O you, you have changed your mind regarding what you granted us!" It was said to Abū ʿAbd Allāh (peace be upon him): "Do you think he feared that [Gabriel] would kill him?" He replied: "No, but he feared that he would strike him a blow that would disgrace him until the Day of Resurrection." And Allah revealed to His Prophet: إِذْ يُوحِي رَبُّكَ إِلَى الْمَلائِكَةِ أَنِّي مَعَكُمْ فَثَبِّتُوا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا سَأُلْقِي فِي قُلُوبِ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا الرُّعْبَ فَاضْرِبُوا فَوْقَ الْأَعْناقِ وَاضْرِبُوا مِنْهُمْ كُلَّ بَنانٍ When your Lord revealed to the angels: ‘I am with you; so make the faithful stand firm. I will cast terror into the hearts of the faithless. So strike above the necks, and strike every fingertip of theirs.’ He said: "The tips of the fingers (أطراف الأصابع)." Quraysh has come with its arrogance and pride, seeking to extinguish the light of Allah. وَيَأْبَى اللهُ إِلَّا أَنْ يُتِمَّ نُورَهُ. But Allah is intent on perfecting His light, though the faithless should be averse. Abū Jahl came out between the two ranks and said: "O Allah, Muḥammad has severed our kinship and brought us something we do not recognize. So, disgrace him this morning." Then Allah revealed to His Messenger: إِنْ تَسْتَفْتِحُوا فَقَدْ جاءَكُمُ الْفَتْحُ وَإِنْ تَنْتَهُوا فَهُوَ خَيْرٌ لَكُمْ وَإِنْ تَعُودُوا نَعُدْ وَلَنْ تُغْنِيَ عَنْكُمْ فِئَتُكُمْ شَيْئاً وَلَوْ كَثُرَتْ وَأَنَّ اللهَ مَعَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ If you sought a judgment, the judgment has certainly come to you. And if you desist, it will be better for you. But if you return, We too will return, and your host will not avail you in any way, though it be numerous, and Allah is with the faithful. Then the Messenger of Allah صلى اللهُ عليه وآله وسلم took a handful of pebbles (حصًى) and threw them into the faces of Quraysh, saying: "May the faces be deformed (شاهت الوجُوه)." Allah then sent winds striking the faces of Quraysh, and the rout occurred.

Then the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "O Allah, let the Pharaoh of this Ummah, Abū Jahl ibn Hishām, not overcome You." Seventy of them were killed and seventy were taken captive. ʿAmr ibn al-Jamūḥ (عمرو بن الجموح) met Abū Jahl. ʿAmr struck Abū Jahl on his thigh, and Abū Jahl struck ʿAmr on his hand, severing it from the upper arm. It hung by the skin. ʿAmr leaned (1) on his hand with his foot, then pulled upward until the skin was cut, and he threw his hand away.

ʿAbd Allāh ibn Masʿūd said: "I reached Abū Jahl while he was writhing in his blood. I said: 'Praise be to Allah Who has disgraced you!' He raised his head and said: 'Allah has only disgraced a slave, the son (1) of a slave woman! To whom belongs the religion and to whom belongs the dominion? Woe to you!' I said: 'To Allah and His Messenger, and I am going to kill you.' I placed my foot on his neck. He said: 'You have climbed (2) a difficult ascent, O little shepherd (رُوَيْعِيَ الغنم)! There is nothing harder than you killing me today. Would that my killing had been carried out only by a man from the Banū al-Muṭṭalib (المطّلبيّين) or a man from the allies (الأحلاف).'" A helmet (بيضة) that was on his head came off, so I killed him. I took his head and brought it to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and said: "O Messenger of Allah, good tidings! This is the head of Abū Jahl ibn Hishām." He prostrated to Allah in gratitude.

Abū Bishr al-Anṣārī (أبو بشر الأنصاري) captured al-ʿAbbās ibn ʿAbd al-Muṭṭalib and ʿAqīl ibn Abī Ṭālib and brought them to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. He صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم asked him: "Did anyone help you against them?" He replied: "Yes, a man wearing white clothes." The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "That was one of the angels." Then the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said to al-ʿAbbās: "Ransom yourself and your nephew." He said: "O Messenger of Allah, I had already embraced Islam, but the people forced me [to come]." The Messenger of Allah said: "Allah knows best about your Islam. If what you mention is true, Allah will reward you for it. But as for your apparent situation, you were against us." Then he said: "O ʿAbbās, you contended with Allah, and He overcame you." Then he said: "Ransom yourself and your nephew." Al-ʿAbbās had taken forty ūqiyyah (اوقية) of gold with him, and the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم had taken it as spoils. When the Messenger of Allah told al-ʿAbbās to ransom himself, he said: "O Messenger of Allah, count this as part of my ransom." The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "No, that is something Allah granted us from you. So, ransom yourself and your nephew." Al-ʿAbbās said: "I have no wealth other than what was taken from me." He said: "Yes, you do. The wealth you left with Umm al-Faḍl (أمّ الفضل) in Mecca, when you told her that if anything happened to you, she should divide it among you." He said to Him: "Will You leave me to beg from people with my hand?" Then Allah revealed to His Messenger concerning this: يا أَيُّهَا النَّبِيُّ قُلْ لِمَنْ فِي أَيْدِيكُمْ مِنَ الْأَسْرى إِنْ يَعْلَمِ اللهُ فِي قُلُوبِكُمْ خَيْراً يُؤْتِكُمْ خَيْراً مِمَّا أُخِذَ مِنْكُمْ وَيَغْفِرْ لَكُمْ وَاللهُ غَفُورٌ رَحِيمٌ. O Prophet, say to the captives who are in your hands: ‘If Allah finds any good in your hearts, He will give you better than what has been taken from you, and He will forgive you, and Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful.’ Then Allah said: وَإِنْ يُرِيدُوا خِيانَتَكَ And if they intend to betray you, regarding ʿAlī, فَقَدْ خانُوا اللهَ مِنْ قَبْلُ they have already betrayed Allah before, regarding you, فَأَمْكَنَ مِنْهُمْ whereupon He gave [you] power over them. Then the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said to ʿAqīl: "O Abū Yazīd, Allah has killed Abū Jahl ibn Hishām, ʿUtbah ibn Rabīʿah, Shaybah ibn Rabīʿah, Munabbih and Nubayh, the sons of al-Ḥajjāj, and Nawfal ibn Khuwaylid. And Suhayl ibn ʿAmr (سهيل بن عمرو), al-Naḍr ibn al-Ḥārith ibn Kaldah (النضر بن الحرث بن كلدة), ʿUqbah ibn Abī Muʿayṭ (عقبة بن أبي معيط), and so-and-so, and so-and-so have been captured." ʿAqīl said: "Then you will not be disputed over in Tihāmah (تهامة). If you have thoroughly defeated the people, [then stop], otherwise, ride upon their shoulders." The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم smiled.

The killed at Badr were seventy, and the captives were seventy. The Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) killed twenty-seven of them and did not take anyone captive. They gathered the captives, distributed them among the camels, and drove them on foot. They collected the spoils. Nine men from the companions of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم were killed, including Saʿd ibn Khaythamah (سعد بن خيثمة), who was one of the leaders (النقباءِ). The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم departed from Badr and stopped at al-Athīl (الأثيل) at sunset, which is six miles from Badr. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم looked at ʿUqbah ibn Abī Muʿayṭ and al-Naḍr ibn al-Ḥārith ibn Kaldah, who were bound together. Al-Naḍr said to ʿUqbah: "O ʿUqbah, you and I are going to be killed." ʿUqbah asked: "From among Quraysh?" He replied: "Yes, because Muḥammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم looked at us with a look in which I saw death." The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "O ʿAlī, bring al-Naḍr and ʿUqbah to me."

Al-Naḍr was a handsome man with hair. ʿAlī (peace be upon him) came, grabbed him by his hair, and dragged him to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. Al-Naḍr said: "O Muḥammad, I ask you by the kinship between us, that you treat me like a man of Quraysh: if you kill them, kill me; if you ransom them, ransom me; and if you release them, release me." The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "There is no kinship between me and you. Islam has severed the kinship. Bring him forward, O ʿAlī, and strike his neck." ʿUqbah said: "O Muḥammad, did you not say that Quraysh should not be killed in cold blood (لا تُصْبر قريش)?" He said: "Are you from Quraysh? You are merely a brute (علج) from the people of Ṣafūriyyah (صفوريّة). You are older in birth than the father you are named after. You are not one of them. Bring him forward, O ʿAlī, and strike his neck." So, he brought him forward and struck his neck.

When the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم killed al-Naḍr and ʿUqbah, the Anṣār feared that all the captives would be killed. They stood before the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and said: "O Messenger of Allah, we have killed seventy and captured seventy. They are your people and your captives. Grant them to us, O Messenger of Allah, and take ransom (الفداء) from them and release them." Then Allah revealed to them: ما كانَ لِنَبِيٍّ أَنْ يَكُونَ لَهُ أَسْرى حَتَّى يُثْخِنَ فِي الْأَرْضِ تُرِيدُونَ عَرَضَ الدُّنْيا وَاللهُ يُرِيدُ الْآخِرَةَ وَاللهُ عَزِيزٌ حَكِيمٌ لَوْ لا كِتابٌ مِنَ اللهِ سَبَقَ لَمَسَّكُمْ فِيما أَخَذْتُمْ عَذابٌ عَظِيمٌ فَكُلُوا مِمَّا غَنِمْتُمْ حَلالاً طَيِّباً It is not for a prophet to have captives until he has thoroughly subdued the land. You desire the fleeting good of this world, while Allah desires the Hereafter, and Allah is all-mighty, all-wise. Had it not been for a prior decree of Allah, there would have surely befallen you a great punishment for what you took. So eat of what you have taken as spoils, [as] lawful and good. So, He permitted them to take ransom and release them, on the condition that in the following year, a number equal to those for whom they took ransom would be killed from among them. They agreed to this condition. The rest of the narration has already passed in Sūrat Āl ʿImrān (سورة آل عمران).

(١٥) يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا إِذا لَقِيتُمُ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا زَحْفاً ** (15) O you who have faith! When you meet the faithless when they are marching, (meaning) numerous (كثيراً), such that their multitude is seen as if they are creeping (يزحفون), meaning they are approaching (يدنون). Al-Qummi (said): meaning some of them approach others (يدنو بعضهم من بعض). فَلا تُوَلُّوهُمُ الْأَدْبارَ ** do not turn your backs on them. by fleeing (بالانهزام).

(١٦) وَمَنْ يُوَلِّهِمْ يَوْمَئِذٍ دُبُرَهُ إِلَّا مُتَحَرِّفاً لِقِتالٍ ** (16) And whoever turns his back on them that day—unless maneuvering for battle (This is) so that he may charge after retreating, in order to make his enemy imagine that he is defeated, and this is one of the stratagems (مكايد) of war. أَوْ مُتَحَيِّزاً إِلى فِئَةٍ ** or joining a company— or joining (منحازاً) another company (فئة) of Muslims so that he may seek assistance from them. فَقَدْ باءَ بِغَضَبٍ مِنَ اللهِ وَمَأْواهُ جَهَنَّمُ وَبِئْسَ الْمَصِيرُ ** he shall incur the wrath of Allah, and his refuge shall be hell, and it is an evil destination. Al-Ayyashi narrated from Imam al-Kazim (peace be upon him) regarding: إِلَّا مُتَحَرِّفاً لِقِتالٍ ** (unless maneuvering for battle). He said: (It means) one who is driving away (متطرّداً), intending to charge (الكرة) back against them. أَوْ مُتَحَيِّزاً ** (or joining a company) means one who is retreating (متأخّراً) to his companions from seeing defeat. So whoever flees until he passes the rank (صف) of his companions فَقَدْ باءَ بِغَضَبٍ مِنَ اللهِ.** (he shall incur the wrath of Allah).

(17) فَلَمْ تَقْتُلُوهُمْ ** (Then you did not kill them) by your strength, meaning: if you boast about killing them, then you did not kill them, وَلكِنَّ اللهَ قَتَلَهُمْ (but Allah killed them) by sending down the angels, casting terror into their hearts, and strengthening your hearts. وَما رَمَيْتَ ** (And you did not cast)—you, O Muhammad—إِذْ رَمَيْتَ وَلكِنَّ اللهَ رَمى ** (when you cast, but Allah cast) since the casting produced that great effect. Al-Qummi (said): It means the pebbles (الحَصى) which the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم carried and cast into the faces of Quraysh, and he said: "May the faces be disfigured (شاهت الوجوه)." (1)

It is narrated: When Quraysh came with their arrogance, Gabriel came to Him and said: "Take a handful of dust and cast it at them." So He said to Ali (عليه السلام): "Give me a handful of pebbles (حصاة) from the valley." So he gave it to Him, and He cast it into their faces and said: "May the faces be disfigured (شاهت الوجوه)." There remained no polytheist (مشرك) except that he was preoccupied with his eyes, so they were routed, and the believers followed them, killing them and taking them captive. Then, when they departed, they began to boast, and a man would say: "I killed," or "I captured." So the verse of casting (آية الرّمي) was revealed for the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم because the form (صورة) of the action was found from him, but the true meaning (معنىِّ) was negated from him, because the effect of it, which does not fall within the capability of humans, was the action of Allah, the Glorified. Thus, it was as if He was the true doer of the casting (الرّمية), and as if it had not originated from the Messenger. And there is another subtle (غامِض) interpretation concerning it.

In al-Ihtijaj, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) in a hadith, he said: "In this verse, the action of the Prophet is named as His action. Do you not see its interpretation (تأويله) is contrary to its literal revelation (تنزيله)?"

Al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Sadiq and al-Sajjad (peace be upon them both) that Ali (peace be upon him) handed the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم the handful [of pebbles] with which He cast into the faces of the polytheists (المشركين). So Allah said: وَما رَمَيْتَ إِذْ رَمَيْتَ وَلكِنَّ اللهَ رَمى (And you did not cast when you cast, but Allah cast).

In al-Khisal, concerning the merits (مناقب) of the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) and their enumeration, he said: "As for the thirty-fifth [merit], the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم directed me on the Day of Badr and said: 'Bring me a handful of pebbles (حصيات) gathered in one place.' So I took them, then I smelled them, and behold, they were pleasant, emanating the fragrance of musk (المسك). I brought them to Him, and He cast them into the faces of the polytheists (المشركين). And those pebbles (الحصيات): four of them were from al-Firdaws (الفردوس), one pebble was from the East (المشرق), one pebble was from the West (المغرب), and one pebble was from beneath the Throne (العرش). With every pebble were one hundred thousand angels as support (مدداً) for us. Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, did not honor anyone with this virtue (الفضيلة) before us or after us."

وَلِيُبْلِيَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ مِنْهُ بَلاءً حَسَناً ** (and that He may test the faithful with a good test from Himself.) And that He may bestow upon them a great favor (نعمة عظيمة) through victory (النّصر), spoils (الغنيمة), and witnessing the signs (الآيات). He did what He did. إِنَّ اللهَ سَمِيعٌ ** (Indeed Allah is all-hearing) of their pleas for help (استغاثتهم) and their supplications (دعائهم), عَلِيمٌ ** (all-knowing) of their intentions (نيّاتهم) and their states (أحوالهم).

(1) i.e., they were made ugly. It is said: shāha yashūhu shawhan (شاه يشوه شوهاً) and shawwaha tashwīhan (شوه تشويهاً), and a man is ashwah (أشوه) and a woman is shawhā’ (شوهاء).

(١٨) ذلِكُمْ ** (That is [the case], and Allah is the frustrater of the stratagems of the faithless.) ذلِكُمْ ** meaning: the objective (الغرض). ذلكم وَأَنَّ اللهَ مُوهِنُ كَيْدِ الْكافِرِينَ ** Meaning that the objective (المقصود) is the testing (إبلاء) of the believers and the weakening (توهين) of the stratagems (كيد) of the faithless. And it was recited (مُوهّن كيدِ) with the idafa (الإِضافة) (genitive construction) and tashdid (التشديد) (doubling of the hā'). (١٩) إِنْ تَسْتَفْتِحُوا فَقَدْ جاءَكُمُ الْفَتْحُ ** (If you sought a judgement, the judgement has certainly come to you.) It is said that the address is directed at the people of Mecca (أهل مكّة) by way of mockery (التهكّم), since it is narrated: That when they intended to depart, they clung to the curtains (أستار) of the Ka'bah and said: "O Allah, grant victory to the superior of the two armies (الجندين), the more guided of the two parties (الفئتين), and the nobler of the two factions (الحزبين)." In al-Majma', in the narration (حديث) of Abu Hamzah: Abu Jahl said: "O Allah, our Lord, our religion is the ancient one (القديم) and the religion of Muhammad is the new one (الحديث). Whichever of the two religions is more beloved to You and more pleasing in Your sight, grant victory to its followers today." And it is narrated: That he said: "Whichever of us is more deserving of abandonment (أهجر) and more severe in severing kinship ties (أقطع للرّحمِ), weaken him (فَاهِنْه) today and destroy him." And it is said that the address is directed at the believers, and likewise, the two interpretations (القولان) apply to what follows it: وَإِنْ تَنْتَهُوا (And if you desist) from disbelief (الكفر), hostility toward the Messenger (معاداة الرَّسُول), laziness (التكاسل) in fighting, and desiring (الرّغبة) what the Messenger reserves for himself (يستأثره), فَهُوَ خَيْرٌ لَكُمْ ** (it will be better for you), because it encompasses the safety (سلامة) of the two abodes (الدّارين) and the best of the two destinations (المنزلين). وَإِنْ تَعُودُوا (But if you revert) to fighting (المحاربة) and laziness (التكاسل), نَعُدْ (We will revert) to granting Him victory (نصره) and condemnation (الإِنكار). وَلَنْ تُغْنِيَ عَنْكُمْ فِئَتُكُمْ ** (and your host will not avail you in any wise), and your group (جماعتكم) will not repel (تدفع) from you anything of benefit (الإِغناءِ) or harm (المضارّ), وَلَوْ كَثُرَتْ (though it be numerous). وَأَنَّ اللهَ مَعَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ** (and Allah is with the believers) through victory (النصر) and assistance (المعونة).

(٢٠) يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا أَطِيعُوا اللهَ وَرَسُولَهُ وَلا تَوَلَّوْا عَنْهُ ** (20) O you who have faith! Obey Allah and His Apostle, and do not turn away from Him from the Messenger وَأَنْتُمْ تَسْمَعُونَ ** while you hear. the Quran and the admonitions, hearing with understanding and affirmation (تَصْدِيق). (٢١) وَلا تَكُونُوا كَالَّذِينَ قالُوا سَمِعْنا ** (21) Do not be like those who say, 'We hear,' they claimed hearing وَهُمْ لا يَسْمَعُونَ ** but they do not hear. a hearing by which they benefit. (٢٢) إِنَّ شَرَّ الدَّوَابِّ عِنْدَ اللهِ الصُّمُ ** (22) Indeed, the worst of beasts in Allah's sight are the deaf concerning the truth الْبُكْمُ الَّذِينَ لا يَعْقِلُونَ ** and the dumb who do not apply reason. to the truth (1) (٢٣) وَلَوْ عَلِمَ اللهُ فِيهِمْ خَيْراً لَأَسْمَعَهُمْ ** (23) Had Allah known any good in them, He would have surely made them hear; a hearing of understanding وَلَوْ أَسْمَعَهُمْ ** and had He made them hear, even though He knew there was no good in them, they would have turned away and not benefited from it وَهُمْ مُعْرِضُونَ ** they would have turned away, being disregardful. due to their obstinacy (2). In al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): It was revealed concerning Banu 'Abd al-Dar; none of them had embraced Islam except Mus'ab ibn 'Umayr and an ally called Suwayt.

(1) Meaning these polytheists (المشركين) who did not benefit from what they heard of the truth (الحقّ), nor did they speak it, nor did they believe it (يَعْتَقِدُونَهُ), nor did they affirm it (يُقِرُّونَ بِهِ). Thus, they are as if they are deaf and dumb; they also do not reflect upon what they hear, so it is as if they did not benefit from their intellects (عُقُولِهِمْ) either, and they became like beasts (الدّوَاب).

(2) And in this is an indication that Allah, the Exalted, does not withhold grace (اللّطْف) from any of those held responsible (المُكَلَّفِين). Rather, He does not grant grace (يَلْطُف) to the one whom He knows will not benefit from it.

(٢٤) يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا اسْتَجِيبُوا لِلَّهِ وَلِلرَّسُولِ إِذا دَعاكُمْ لِما يُحْيِيكُمْ. (24) O you who have faith! Answer Allah and the Apostle when He calls you to that which gives you life.

— by obedience (بالطاعة) — when the Messenger (الرسول) calls you to that which gives you life.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): It was revealed concerning the wilayah (ولاية) of Ali (peace be upon him). And al-Qummi (said): The life (الحيوة) is Paradise (الجنّة). And narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): This verse concerns the wilayah (ولاية) of Ali ibn Abi Talib (peace be upon him), for your following of him and his wilayah (ولايته) is more unifying for your affair (أمركم) and more enduring for justice (العدل) among you.

**وَاعْلَمُوا أَنَّ اللهَ يَحُولُ بَيْنَ الْمَرْءِ وَقَلْبِهِ ** and know that Allah intervenes between a man and his heart,

— He possesses the power to turn the hearts (تقلّب القلوب) from one state to another. Al-Qummi (said): (It means) that He intervenes between him and what he desires. And narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): يَحُولُ بَيْنَ ** (He intervenes between) the believer and his disobedience (معصيته) so that it does not lead him to the Fire, and between the disbeliever (الكافر) and his obedience (طاعته) so that he does not complete faith (الإيمان) through it. He (peace be upon him) said: And know that deeds (الأعمال) are judged by their conclusions (خواتيمها). In al-Tawhid and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding this verse: يَحُولُ ** (He intervenes) between him and knowing that falsehood (الباطل) is truth (حقّ). In al-Majma' and al-Ayyashi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): Its meaning is that the heart (القلب) never becomes certain that the truth (الحقّ) is falsehood (باطل), nor does the heart ever become certain that falsehood (الباطل) is truth (حقّ). And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): It is that he desires a thing with his hearing, his sight, his tongue, and his hand. However, if he approaches something that he desires, he does not approach it except that his heart is disapproving (منكر), not accepting what he approaches, knowing that the truth (الحقّ) is not in it. And narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): This is the thing that a man desires with his heart, his hearing, and his sight, and his soul does not yearn for anything other than that. Thus, intervention has occurred between him and his heart, except for that thing.

**وَأَنَّهُ إِلَيْهِ تُحْشَرُونَ ** and that you will be mustered toward Him.

— so He will recompense you for your deeds (أعمالكم).

(٢٥) وَاتَّقُوا فِتْنَةً لا تُصِيبَنَّ الَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا مِنْكُمْ خَاصَّةً ** (25) And beware of a trial which will not afflict exclusively those who commit injustice among you. Rather, it will encompass them and others, such as complacency (المداهنة) regarding enjoining the right and forbidding the wrong, the division of unity (افتراق الكلمة), and the emergence of innovations (البدع). And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding this verse, He said: "A trial (فتنة) afflicted the people after Allah took His Prophet صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم, until they abandoned Ali (peace be upon him) and pledged allegiance to someone else. This is the trial (الفتنة) by which they were tested, even though the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم had commanded them to follow Ali (peace be upon him) and the Successors (الأوصياء) from the Family of Muhammad (prayers of Allah be upon them)." And in al-Majma, narrated from Ali and al-Baqir (peace be upon them both): that they both recited (لَتُصِيبَنَّ) (it will surely afflict). And from Ibn Abbas: When it was revealed, the Prophet صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "Whoever wrongs Ali (peace be upon him) in this position of mine after my death, it is as if he has denied my prophethood and the prophethood of the Prophets before me." And al-Qummi [said]: It was revealed concerning Talhah and al-Zubayr when they fought the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) and wronged him. وَاعْلَمُوا أَنَّ اللهَ شَدِيدُ الْعِقابِ.** (25) and know that Allah is severe in retribution.

(26) وَاذْكُرُوا إِذْ أَنْتُمْ قَلِيلٌ مُسْتَضْعَفُونَ فِي الْأَرْضِ تَخافُونَ أَنْ يَتَخَطَّفَكُمُ ** (1) (And remember when you were few, abased in the land, afraid that the people might abduct you) النَّاسُ فَآواكُمْ وَأَيَّدَكُمْ بِنَصْرِهِ وَرَزَقَكُمْ مِنَ الطَّيِّباتِ ** (people, then He sheltered you, and strengthened you with His help, and provided you with good things) from the spoils (الغنائم) لَعَلَّكُمْ تَشْكُرُونَ ** (so that you may be grateful.) for these blessings (النّعم). Al-Qummi: It was revealed specifically concerning Quraysh, and this is also narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him).

(٢٧) يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لا تَخُونُوا اللهَ وَالرَّسُولَ وَتَخُونُوا أَماناتِكُمْ وَأَنْتُمْ تَعْلَمُونَ (27) O you who have faith! Do not betray Allah and the Apostle, nor betray your trusts while you know. that you are betraying.

In al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir and al-Sadiq (peace be upon them both): It was revealed concerning Abu Lubabah ibn 'Abd al-Mundhir al-Ansari. This was because the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم besieged the Jews of Banu Qurayzah for twenty-one nights. So they asked the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم for a truce (الصلح) based on what their brethren from Banu al-Nadir had agreed upon: that they should travel to their brethren in Adhri'at and Ariha in the land of Sham (الشام). But the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم refused to grant them that, except that they submit to the judgment of Sa'd ibn Mu'adh. They said: "Send Abu Lubabah to us," and he was a sincere advisor (مناصحاً) to them because his family, wealth, and children were with them. So the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم sent him. He came to them, and they said: "What do you think, O Abu Lubabah? Should we submit to the judgment of Sa'd ibn Mu'adh?" Abu Lubabah pointed with his hand to his throat, indicating that it was slaughter (الذّبح), so they should not do it. Then Jibril (جبرئيل) came to Him and informed Him of that. Abu Lubabah said: "By Allah, my feet had not moved from their place until I knew that I had betrayed Allah and His Messenger." So the Ayah was revealed concerning him. When it was revealed, he tied himself to a pillar (سارية) (1) from the pillars of the Mosque and said: "By Allah, I will not taste food or drink until I die or Allah accepts my repentance." He remained for seven days, during which he tasted neither food nor drink, until he fell unconscious. Then Allah accepted his repentance. It was said to him: "O Abu Lubabah, your repentance has been accepted." He said: "No, by Allah, I will not untie myself until the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم is the one who unties me." So He came to him and untied him with His own hand. Then Abu Lubabah said: "Indeed, part of the completeness of my repentance is that I abandon the dwelling of my people where I committed the sin, and that I divest myself of my wealth." The Prophet صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "A third is sufficient for you, that you give it in charity (تتصدّق به)."

And al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): The betrayal of Allah and the Messenger is disobedience to them both. As for the betrayal of the trust (خيانة الأمانة), every human being is entrusted (مأمون) with what Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, has made obligatory upon him. He said: It was revealed concerning Abu Lubabah ibn 'Abd al-Mundhir, so the wording (لفظ) of the Ayah is general (عامّ) but its meaning (معناها) is specific (خاصّ). He said: And it was revealed during the Battle of Banu Qurayzah in the fifth year of the Hijrah, and it was written in this Surah along with the accounts of Badr, which occurred sixteen months after the arrival of the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم in Medina. And it was revealed along with the Ayah in Surah al-Tawbah, His saying: وَآخَرُونَ اعْتَرَفُوا بِذُنُوبِهِمْ (And others have confessed to their sins) which was revealed concerning Abu Lubabah. He said: This is the proof that the arrangement (التأليف) is contrary to what Allah revealed to His Prophet. Then he mentioned this story there, as will follow.

(1) Al-Takhṭṭuf (التخطّف) is seizing quickly, snatching away. It is said: takhṭṭafa, khaṭafa, and ikhtaṭafa (تخطّف وخطف واختطف), meaning: the polytheists (المشركون) from the Arabs will snatch you away if you leave it (Medina). And it was said that He means by 'the people' (النّاس) the disbelievers of Quraysh (كفّار قريش). And it was said: Persia and Rome (فارس والرّوم). فَآواكُمْ (and gave you shelter), meaning: He made for you a refuge (مأوىً) to which you return, meaning Medina, the Abode of Migration (دار الهجرة).
