(٢٨) وَاعْلَمُوا أَنَّما أَمْوالُكُمْ وَأَوْلادُكُمْ فِتْنَةٌ (٢) (28) And know that your possessions and children are only a trial Because they distract you from the remembrance of Allah. وَاللهُ عِنْدَهُ أَجْرٌ عَظِيمٌ ** And Allah—with Him is a great reward** for whoever prefers the pleasure of Allah over them.

In al-Majma', narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): Let none of you say: "O Allah, I seek refuge in You from the trial (الفتنة)," because there is no one except that he is encompassed by a trial (فتنة). Rather, whoever seeks refuge, let him seek refuge from the misleading trials (مضلّات الفتن). For Allah, the Glorified, says: أَنَّما أَمْوالُكُمْ وَأَوْلادُكُمْ فِتْنَةٌ. that your possessions and children are only a trial.

footnote (١) *Al-Sāriyah* (*السارية*): The pillar (*الأسطوانة*).

footnote (٢) Meaning: a tribulation (*بلاء*) and a hardship (*محنة*) and a cause for your falling into great crimes. That is, He, the Glorified, tests them with possessions and children so that the one who is content with the division (*قسمة*) may be distinguished from the one who is not content with it, even though He, the Glorified, knows them better than they know themselves, but so that the actions may become manifest by which one deserves reward (*الثواب*) and punishment (*العقاب*).

(٢٩) يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا إِنْ تَتَّقُوا اللهَ يَجْعَلْ لَكُمْ فُرْقاناً ** (O you who have faith! If you are wary of Allah, He will grant you a criterion) Guidance (هداية) in your hearts by which you distinguish between truth (الحقّ) and falsehood (الباطل). Al-Qummi said: It means the knowledge (العلم) by which you distinguish between truth and falsehood. وَيُكَفِّرْ عَنْكُمْ سَيِّئاتِكُمْ ** (and absolve you of your misdeeds) and He covers them (i.e., the misdeeds). وَيَغْفِرْ لَكُمْ ** (and forgive you) by overlooking (بالتجاوز) and pardoning (والعفو) them. وَاللهُ ذُو الْفَضْلِ الْعَظِيمِ.** (and Allah is the Possessor of great grace.)

And when the faithless plotted against you (8:30). And remember when the faithless plotted against you (8:30)—meaning Quraysh. He reminded him of that so that he might thank Allah for His favor upon him in his deliverance—to detain you (8:30) by imprisonment, or slay you (8:30) with their swords, or expel you (8:30) from Mecca. And they plotted, and Allah plotted, and Allah is the best of plotters (8:30)—by repelling their plot and recompensing them for it.

In al-Ayyashi, narrated from one of the two Imams (peace be upon them both): Quraysh gathered, and people from every clan (بطن) came out, then they proceeded to Dar al-Nadwah (دار النّدوة) to consult on what they should do with the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. Suddenly, there was an old man standing at the door. When they went to him to enter, he said: "Let me enter with you." They asked: "Who are you, O Sheikh (شيخ)?" He replied: "I am a Sheikh from Mudar (مضرَ), and I have an opinion I can offer you." So they entered and sat down, consulting while he sat there. They agreed unanimously to expel him. He said: "This is not a sound opinion for you. If you expel him, people will rally against you and fight you." They said: "You speak the truth, this is not a sound opinion." Then they consulted again and agreed unanimously to bind him. He said: "This is not a sound opinion. If you do this, Muhammad is a man with a sweet tongue; he will corrupt your sons and your servants, and it will not benefit any of you if his brother, son, and wife abandon him." Then they consulted again and agreed unanimously to kill him: people from every clan (بطن) would come out with a drawn sword (بشاهر) and strike him all together with their swords near the Ka'bah. Then he recited this verse: وَإِذْ يَمْكُرُ بِكَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا (And when the faithless plotted against you).

Al-Qummi narrated: It was revealed in Mecca before the Hijrah (الهجرة). The reason for its revelation was that when the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم openly proclaimed the call (الدّعوة) in Mecca, the Aws (الأوس) and the Khazraj (الخزرج) came to him. The Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم said to them: "Will you protect me (1) and be a neighbor (جاراً) to me until I recite to you the Book of my Lord, and your reward from Allah will be Paradise (الجنّة)?" They said: "Yes, take for your Lord and for yourself whatever you wish." He said to them: "Your appointment is at al-Aqabah (العقبة) (1) on the middle night of the nights of al-Tashriq (التشريق)." So they performed the Hajj (حجّوا) and returned to Mina (منى). There were many people among them who had performed the Hajj. When it was the second of the days of al-Tashriq.

The Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم said to them: "When night falls, attend the house of Abd al-Muttalib (عبد المطلب) at al-Aqabah (العَقَبة). Do not wake a sleeper, and let one person slip away (2) after another." So seventy men from the Aws and the Khazraj came and entered the house. The Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم said to them: "Will you protect me and grant me asylum until I recite to you the Book of my Lord, and your reward from Allah will be Paradise (الجنّة)?"

Sa'd ibn Zurarah (سعد بن زرارة), al-Bara' ibn Ma'rur (البراء بن معرور), and Abdullah ibn Hizām (عبد الله بن حزام) said: "Yes, O Messenger of Allah, stipulate for your Lord and for yourself whatever you wish." He said: "As for what I stipulate for my Lord, it is that you worship Him and associate nothing with Him. And I stipulate for myself that you protect me from what you protect yourselves, and that you protect my family from what you protect your families and your children." They asked: "And what shall be our reward for that?" He replied: "Paradise (الجنّة) in the Hereafter, and you shall rule the Arabs and the non-Arabs (العجم) shall submit to you in this world, and you shall be kings in Paradise." They said: "We are satisfied."

Then He said: "Bring forth from among you twelve leaders (نقيباً) who shall be witnesses (شُهَدَاءَ) over you concerning this, just as Moses took twelve leaders from the Children of Israel to be witnesses over them concerning this." Gabriel (جبرئيل) pointed to him and said: "This is a leader (نقيب), and this is a leader." Nine were from the Khazraj and three were from the Aws. Those from the Khazraj were Sa'd ibn Zurarah (سعد بن زرارة), al-Bara' ibn Ma'rur (البراء بن معرور), Abdullah ibn Hizām (عبد الله بن حزام)—the father of Jabir ibn Abdullah (جابر بن عبد الله)—Rafi' ibn Malik (رافع بن مالك), Sa'd ibn Ubadah (سعد بن عبادة), al-Mundhir ibn Amr (المنذر بن عمر), Abdullah ibn Rawahah (عبد الله بن رواحة), Sa'd ibn al-Rabi' (سعد بن الربيع), and Ubadah ibn al-Samit (عبادة بن الصامت). And those from the Aws were Abu al-Haytham ibn al-Tayyihan (أبو الهيثم بن التّيهان)—who was from Yemen (اليمن)—Asad ibn Husayn (أسد بن حصين), and Sa'd ibn Khaythamah (سعد بن خيثمة).

When they gathered and pledged allegiance (بايعوا) to the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم, Iblis (إبليس) cried out: "O assembly of Quraysh and the Arabs! This is Muhammad صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم and the apostates (الصباة) from the people of Yathrib (يثرب) at Jamrat al-Aqabah (جمرة العقبة), pledging allegiance to him for war against you!" He made the people of Mina (منى) hear, and Quraysh became agitated and advanced with weapons.

The Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم heard the call (النّداءَ) and said to the Ansar (الأنصار): "Disperse." They said: "O Messenger of Allah, if You command us to charge them with our swords, we shall do so." The Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم said to them: "I have not been commanded to do that, nor has Allah permitted me to fight them." They asked: "Will You then leave with us?" He said: "I await the command of Allah." Then Quraysh came, all of them, having taken up arms. Hamzah (حمزة) and the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) came out, both carrying swords, and stood at al-Aqabah. When Quraysh saw them, they asked: "What is this gathering you have come for?" Hamzah said: "We have not gathered, and there is no one here. By Allah, no one shall pass this Aqabah (العقبة) except that I strike him with my sword." So they returned to Mecca and said: "We are not safe from having our affair corrupted and one of the elders (مشايخ) of Quraysh entering the religion of Muhammad صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم." So they gathered in al-Nadwah (النّدوة). No one used to enter Dar al-Nadwah (دار النّدوة) except those who had reached forty years of age. Forty men from the elders of Quraysh entered, and Iblis (إبليس) came in the form of an old man. The doorkeeper asked him: "Who are you?" He replied: "I am a Sheikh (شيخ) from the people of Najd (نجد). You will not lack a sound opinion from me. When I heard of your gathering concerning the matter of this man, I came to advise you." He said: "Enter." So Iblis entered. When they had taken their seats.

Abu Jahl (أبو جهل) said: "O assembly of Quraysh! There has been no one among the Arabs more honored than us. We are the people of Allah. The Arabs come to us twice a year and honor us, and we are in the Sanctuary (حرم) of Allah, where no ambitious person dares to covet us. We remained thus until Muhammad ibn Abdullah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم grew up among us. We used to call him al-Amin (الأمين) due to his righteousness, tranquility, and truthful speech. But when he reached the status he reached, and we honored him, he claimed that he is the Messenger of Allah and that news of the heavens comes to him. He has belittled our wisdom (أحلامنا), insulted our gods (آلهتنا), corrupted our youth, divided our unity, and claimed that whoever died among our ancestors is in the Fire. Nothing greater than this has been inflicted upon us. I have formed an opinion concerning him." They asked: "What is your opinion?" He said: "My opinion is that we secretly send a man from among us to kill him. If Banu Hashim (بنو هاشم) demand blood money for him, we will give them ten blood-wits (ديات)."

The wicked one (Iblis) said: "This is a wicked opinion." They asked: "How so?" He said: "Because the killer of Muhammad will inevitably be killed. Who among you is this person who will sacrifice himself for death? For if Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم is killed, Banu Hashim and their allies from Khuzā'ah (خزاعة) will rally together, and Banu Hashim will not be satisfied to let the killer of Muhammad صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم walk upon the earth. Thus, wars will break out among you in your Sanctuary (حَرَمكم) and you will perish." Another one among them said: "Then I have another opinion." He asked: "What is it?" He said: "We will confine him in his house and throw his food to him until the uncertainty of death (ريب المنون) overtakes him, and he dies just as Zuhayr (زهير), al-Nabighah (النّابغة), and Imru' al-Qays (امرؤ القيس) died."

Iblis said: "This is more wicked than the previous one." He asked: "How so?" He said: "Because Banu Hashim will not be satisfied with that. When a season of the Arab seasons (مواسم العرب) comes, they will seek help from them, and they will gather against you and bring him out." Another one among them said: "No, rather we will expel him from our lands, and we will devote ourselves to the worship of our gods (آلهتنا)." Iblis said: "This is more wicked than the two preceding opinions."

They asked: "How is that?" He said: "Because you are deliberately sending the most handsome of people in countenance, the most eloquent of people in speech, and the most articulate of them in dialect, and you carry him off to the deserts (بوادي) of the Arabs. He will deceive them and subjugate them with his tongue, and he will not surprise you except that he has filled them against you with cavalry (خيلاً) and infantry (رَجِلاً)." So they remained bewildered. Then they said to Iblis: "What is the opinion concerning him, O Sheikh?" He said: "There is only one opinion concerning him." They asked: "What is it?" He said: "One man from every clan (بطن) of Quraysh should gather, and there should be a man from Banu Hashim among them. They should take a knife, or an iron weapon, or a sword, and enter upon him and strike him all together with a single blow, so that his blood is dispersed among all of Quraysh. Then Banu Hashim will not be able to demand blood money for him, since they participated in it. If they ask you to give the blood-wit (الدّية), give them three blood-wits." They said: "Yes, ten blood-wits."

Then they said: "The opinion is the opinion of the Sheikh of Najd (النجدي)." So they gathered, and Abu Lahab (أبو لهب), the Prophet's uncle, joined them in this. Gabriel (جبرئيل) descended upon the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم and informed him that Quraysh had gathered in Dar al-Nadwah (دار الندوة) plotting against him. And concerning this, He revealed to him: And when the faithless plotted against you to detain you, or slay you, or expel you. And they plotted, and Allah plotted, and Allah is the best of plotters (8:30). Quraysh agreed to enter upon him at night and kill him. They went out to the Mosque, whistling (يصفرون) and clapping (يصفقون), and circumambulating the House (يطوفون بالبيت). Then Allah revealed: And their prayer at the House is nothing but whistling (8:35) (1) and clapping (8:35). Al-Muka' (المكاء) is whistling (التصفير), and al-Tasdiyah (التصدية) is the clapping of hands. This verse is connected (معطوفة) to His saying: وَإِذْ يَمْكُرُ بِكَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا (And when the faithless plotted against you), although it was written after many verses.

When the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم reached evening, and Quraysh came to enter upon him, Abu Lahab said: "I will not let you enter upon him at night, for there are children and women in the house, and we are not safe from an accidental hand falling upon them. So we shall guard him tonight, and when morning comes, we shall enter upon him." So they slept around the chamber (حجرة) of the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم. The Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم commanded that a bed be prepared for him, and it was prepared. He said to Ali ibn Abi Talib (peace be upon him): "Redeem me with your soul (أفدني بنفسك)." He said: "Yes, O Messenger of Allah." He said: "Sleep on my bed and cover yourself with my cloak (بردتي)."

So Ali (peace be upon him) slept on the bed of the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم and covered himself with His cloak. Gabriel (جبرئيل) came and took the hand of the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم and led Him out past Quraysh while they were sleeping, and He recited to them: And We have put a barrier before them and a barrier behind them, and We have blindfolded them, so they do not see (36:9). Gabriel said to Him: "Take the path of Thawr (ثور)," which is a mountain on the road to Mina (منى) that has a hump like the hump of a bull (الثور). So He entered the cave (الغار), and what happened concerning Him happened.

When morning came, Quraysh rushed to the chamber (الحجرة) and headed for the bed. Ali leaped up in their faces and asked: "What is your business?" They asked him: "Where is Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم?" He said: "Did you appoint me as a guard (رقيباً) over him? Did you not say you would expel him from our lands? He has already left you." So they began to strike him and say: "You have been deceiving us all night!" Then they dispersed into the mountains. There was a man among them from Khuzā'ah (خزاعة) called Abu Kuruz (أبو كرز) who tracked footprints (يقفو الآثار). They said: "O Abu Kuruz, today is the day!" So he stopped them at the door of the chamber of the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم and said: "This is the footprint (قدم) of Muhammad صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم. By Allah, it is the sister of the footprint that is at the Maqam (المقام)."

Abu Bakr had met the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم, and He took him back with Him. Abu Kuruz said: "And this is the footprint of the son of Abu Quhafah (أبي قحافة) or his father." Then he said: "And here is the trace (عير) of the son of Abu Quhafah." He continued with them until he stopped them at the mouth of the cave (الغار). Then he said: "They have not gone past this place. Either they have ascended to the sky (السّماءَ) or entered beneath the earth." And Allah sent the spider (العنكبوت), which wove a web over the mouth of the cave. Then a horseman from the angels (الملائكة) came and stood at the mouth of the cave and said: "There is no one in the cave." So they dispersed into the mountain paths (الشعاب). Thus, He diverted them from the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم, and then He permitted His Prophet to emigrate (الهجرة) from it.

وَإِذا تُتْلى عَلَيْهِمْ آياتُنا قالُوا قَدْ سَمِعْنا لَوْ نَشاءُ لَقُلْنا مِثْلَ هذا ٣١ (And when Our signs are recited to them, they say, 'We have heard. If we wished, we would have surely said the like of this.') It was said that its speaker was Al-Nadr ibn Al-Harith ibn Kaldah. He was captured on the Day of Badr, and the Prophet صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم killed him as a captive (صبراً) by the hand of Ali عليه السلام. He only said it out of arrogance (صلفاً). And this is the limit of their obstinacy (مكابرتهم) and the excess of their defiance (عنادهم). Since if they were able to do that, what prevented them from willing it, when He had challenged them and reproached them with inability (بالعجز) for ten years? Then He fought them with the sword, and they did not oppose anything other than it, despite their excessive eagerness to defeat Him and overcome Him. إِنْ هذا إِلَّا أَساطِيرُ الْأَوَّلِينَ ** (This is nothing but the myths of the ancients.) What the ancients wrote down of stories (القصص). It was said Al-Nadr also said it. And that is because he brought the story (حديث) of Rustam and Isfandiyar from the lands of Persia (فارس) and claimed that this [the Quran] was like that [the stories].

** وَإِذْ قالُوا اللهُمَّ إِنْ كانَ هذا هُوَ الْحَقَّ مِنْ عِنْدِكَ فَأَمْطِرْ عَلَيْنا حِجارَةً مِنَ السَّماءِ أَوِ ائْتِنا بِعَذابٍ أَلِيمٍ ** (32) And when they said, 'O Allah, if this is the truth from Your presence, then rain down upon us stones from the sky, or bring us a painful punishment.' It was said that this is also from the speech of al-Nadr, and it is more extreme in denial (الجحود). He intended by it mockery (التهكم) and showing complete certainty (الجزم التام) that it is false. And al-Qummi said that Abu Jahl said it. And in al-Kafi: al-Harith ibn 'Amr al-Fihri said it. And in al-Majma': al-Nu'man ibn al-Harith said it, as will come later, all of them.

(٣٣) وَما كانَ اللهُ لِيُعَذِّبَهُمْ وَأَنْتَ فِيهِمْ وَما كانَ اللهُ مُعَذِّبَهُمْ وَهُمْ يَسْتَغْفِرُونَ (٣٣) (But Allah would not punish them while you are among them, nor will Allah punish them while they seek forgiveness.) A clarification of the reason for granting them respite and delaying the answer to their prayer.

(٣٤) وَما لَهُمْ أَلَّا يُعَذِّبَهُمُ اللهُ وَهُمْ يَصُدُّونَ عَنِ الْمَسْجِدِ الْحَرامِ ** (And what plea do they have that Allah should not punish them, when they bar [people] from the Sacred Mosque?) For they compelled the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم and the believers to emigrate (الهجرة) and besieged them in the year of al-Hudaybiyyah. وَما كانُوا أَوْلِياءَهُ (nor were they its guardians), deserving the guardianship (ولاية) of its affair despite their polytheism (شرك). This is a refutation of their saying, "We are the guardians (ولاة) of the House (البيت) and the Sanctuary (الحرم)." إِنْ أَوْلِياؤُهُ إِلَّا الْمُتَّقُونَ ** (Its guardians are none but the Godwary), those who are Godwary (المتقون) of polytheism (الشرك), who do not worship anyone other than Him therein.

In al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir عليه السلام: Its meaning is: And the guardians (أولياء) of the Sacred Mosque (المسجد الحرام) are none but the Godwary (إِلَّا الْمُتَّقُونَ).

And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام: وَما كانُوا أَوْلِياءَهُ ** (nor were they its guardians) means the guardians (أولياء) of the House (البيت), meaning the polytheists (المشركين). إِنْ أَوْلِياؤُهُ إِلَّا الْمُتَّقُونَ (Its guardians are none but the Godwary), because wherever they are, they are more deserving of it than the polytheists. وَلكِنَّ أَكْثَرَهُمْ لا يَعْلَمُونَ (but most of them do not know) that they have no guardianship (ولاية) over it.

In al-Qummi: It was revealed when the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم said to Quraysh: "Indeed, Allah has sent me to kill all the kings of the world and bring the kingship (الملك) to you. So respond to what I call you to, and through it you will rule the Arabs, the non-Arabs (العجم) will submit to you, and you will be kings in Paradise (الجنّة)."

Then Abu Jahl said: اللهُمَّ إِنْ كانَ هذا ** (O Allah, if this) which Muhammad says هُوَ الْحَقَّ مِنْ عِنْدِكَ فَأَمْطِرْ عَلَيْنا حِجارَةً مِنَ السَّماءِ أَوِ ائْتِنا بِعَذابٍ أَلِيمٍ ** (is indeed the truth from You, then rain down stones upon us from the sky, or bring us a painful punishment) out of envy (حَسَداً) for the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم. Then he said: "We and Banu Hashim were like two horses in a race (رهان). We carry when they carry, we stab when they stab, and we send delegations (نوفد) (1) when they send delegations. When the riders were equal between us and them, one of them said, 'Among us is a Prophet.' We are not pleased that this should be among Banu Hashim and not among Banu Makhzum." Then he said, "Your forgiveness (غفرانك), O Allah (اللَّهُمَّ)." So Allah revealed concerning that: وَما كانَ اللهُ لِيُعَذِّبَهُمْ وَأَنْتَ فِيهِمْ وَما كانَ اللهُ مُعَذِّبَهُمْ وَهُمْ يَسْتَغْفِرُونَ ** (But Allah would not punish them while you are among them, nor will Allah punish them while they seek forgiveness) when he said, "Your forgiveness (غفرانك), O Allah (اللَّهُمَّ)."

When they intended to kill the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم and expelled him from Mecca, Allah said: وَما لَهُمْ أَلَّا يُعَذِّبَهُمُ اللهُ وَهُمْ يَصُدُّونَ عَنِ الْمَسْجِدِ الْحَرامِ وَما كانُوا أَوْلِياءَهُ (And what plea do they have that Allah should not punish them, when they bar [people] from the Sacred Mosque, nor were they its guardians)—meaning Quraysh, they were not the guardians (أولياء) of Mecca—إِنْ أَوْلِياؤُهُ إِلَّا الْمُتَّقُونَ (Its guardians are none but the Godwary), meaning you and your companions, O Muhammad. So Allah punished them on the Day of Badr, and they were killed.

In al-Kafi, narrated from Abu Basir, who said: While the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم was sitting, the Commander of the Faithful (أمير المؤمنين) approached. The Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم said to him: "Indeed, in you is a resemblance to Jesus son of Mary (عيسى بن مريم). Were it not that groups among my Ummah would say about you what the Christians (النصارى) said about Jesus son of Mary, I would say a word about you such that you would not pass by any gathering of people except that they would take the dust from beneath your feet, seeking blessing (البركة) thereby."

He said: Then the two Bedouins (الأعرابيان), Mughirah ibn Shu'bah, and a number of Quraysh who were with them became angry and said: "He was not satisfied to strike a similitude for his cousin except with Jesus son of Mary." So Allah revealed to His Prophet, saying: وَلَمَّا ضُرِبَ ابْنُ مَرْيَمَ مَثَلاً إِذا قَوْمُكَ مِنْهُ يَصِدُّونَ (٥٧) وَقالُوا أَآلِهَتُنا خَيْرٌ أَمْ هُوَ ما ضَرَبُوهُ لَكَ إِلَّا جَدَلاً بَلْ هُمْ قَوْمٌ خَصِمُونَ (٥٨) إِنْ هُوَ إِلَّا عَبْدٌ أَنْعَمْنا عَلَيْهِ وَجَعَلْناهُ مَثَلاً لِبَنِي إِسْرائِيلَ (٥٩) وَلَوْ نَشاءُ لَجَعَلْنا مِنْكُمْ (And when the son of Mary was cited as a parable, behold, your people raised a clamour at it, and they said, 'Are our gods better, or is he?' They cite him to you only for the sake of argument. Indeed, they are a contentious lot. He is only a servant whom We blessed, and whom We made an example for the Children of Israel. And had We wished, We would have made among you)—meaning among Banu Hashim—مَلائِكَةً فِي الْأَرْضِ يَخْلُفُونَ (٦٠) (angels succeeding [one another] on the earth.)

He said: Then al-Harith ibn 'Amr al-Fihri became angry and said: "O Allah (اللَّهُمَّ), if this is indeed the truth from You, that Banu Hashim will inherit Heraclius (هرقلا) (1) after Heraclius, then send down عَلَيْنا حِجارَةً مِنَ السَّماءِ أَوِ ائْتِنا بِعَذابٍ أَلِيمٍ (stones upon us from the sky, or bring us a painful punishment)." So Allah revealed the statement of al-Harith, and this Ayah was revealed: ما كانَ اللهُ لِيُعَذِّبَهُمْ وَأَنْتَ فِيهِمْ وَما كانَ اللهُ مُعَذِّبَهُمْ وَهُمْ يَسْتَغْفِرُونَ ** (But Allah would not punish them while you are among them, nor will Allah punish them while they seek forgiveness). Then He said to him, "O son of 'Amr, either you repent or you depart." So he called for his riding beast (راحلته) and mounted it. When he reached the outskirts (بظهر) of Medina, a large stone (جندلة) struck him and crushed his skull (هامته). The Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم said to the hypocrites (المنافقين) around him: "Go to your companion, for what he sought to hasten has come to him." Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said: وَاسْتَفْتَحُوا وَخابَ كُلُّ جَبَّارٍ عَنِيدٍ (١٥)** (And they sought a judgement, and every obstinate tyrant failed.)

In al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام, from his forefathers عليهم السلام: When the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم appointed 'Ali on the Day of Ghadir Khumm, he said: "Of whomsoever I am the Mawla, 'Ali is his Mawla." This spread throughout the lands. Then al-Nu'man ibn al-Harith al-Fihri came to the Prophet and said: "You commanded us, from Allah, to testify that there is no god but Allah and that you are the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم, and you commanded us to perform Jihad, Hajj, fasting (الصّوم), prayer (الصّلوة), and Zakat, and we accepted them. Then you were not satisfied with us until you appointed this youth and said, 'Of whomsoever I am the Mawla, 'Ali is his Mawla.' Is this something from you or a command from Allah?" He said: "By Allah, besides Whom there is no god, this is from Allah." Al-Nu'man ibn al-Harith turned away, saying: اللهُمَّ إِنْ كانَ هذا هُوَ الْحَقَّ مِنْ عِنْدِكَ فَأَمْطِرْ عَلَيْنا حِجارَةً مِنَ السَّماءِ ** (O Allah, if this is indeed the truth from You, then rain down stones upon us from the sky). So Allah struck him with a stone on his head and killed him, and the Almighty Allah revealed: سَأَلَ سائِلٌ بِعَذابٍ واقِعٍ (١)** (An asker asked for a punishment sure to befall.)

In al-Kafi, narrated from Abu Basir, who said: The Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "Indeed, for you there is good in my life and good in my death." He said: It was asked, "O Messenger of Allah, as for your life, we know [the good], but what is the good for us in your death?" He replied: "As for my life, Allah says: **وَما كانَ اللهُ لِيُعَذِّبَهُمْ وَأَنْتَ فِيهِمْ ** (But Allah would not punish them while you are among them). And as for my death, your deeds are presented to me, and I seek forgiveness for you."

And in al-Qummi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Baqir عليه السلام: [A narration] close to it, and he said at the end of it: "Indeed, your deeds are presented to me every Thursday and Monday. Whatever good deed there is, I praise Allah for it, and whatever evil deed there is, I seek forgiveness from Allah for you."

In Nahj al-Balaghah: There were two safeguards (أمانان) on Earth against the punishment of Allah. One of them has been removed, and the other is with you, so hold fast to it. As for the safeguard that was removed, it is the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم. As for the remaining safeguard, it is seeking forgiveness (الاستغفار)." Then he recited الْآيَةَ. (the Ayah).

And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام: The Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم and seeking forgiveness (الاستغفار) were two fortresses (حصنين) for you against punishment. The greater of the two fortresses has passed, and seeking forgiveness remains, so increase in it, for it is an eraser (ممحاة) of sins. And if you wish, read [the Quran]." Then he recited الْآيَةَ. (the Ayah).

(1) A boastful (صَلِف) cloud, much thunder, little water. In the proverb: "Perhaps there is boastfulness (صَلِف) beneath the thunder," which is applied to one who threatens but does not follow through, or to the miser who excessively praises himself while having no good to offer.

(2) And the Wafd (الوفد) are the people who gather and arrive in the lands; their singular is Wafid (وافد). The Wafid is also the leading camel (السّابق من الإِبل), and from this comes the saying that the Imam of the people is their Wafid, meaning their forerunner (سابقهم) to Allah, so put forward the best among you.

(3) Heraclius (هرقل), on the measure of Khandaf (خندف), is the name of the King of Rome (ملك الرّوم). Al-Jawhari said that it is also pronounced Hiraql (هرقل) on the measure of Dimashq (دمشق). In al-Majma', he said: Heraclius (هرقل) and Dighatir (ضغاطر) were two kings of the Romans (الروم). Dighatir embraced Islam and called the Romans to Islam, so they killed him. As for Heraclius, he was stingy with his kingship (ملكه) and fought the Muslims at Mu'tah and Tabuk. It is probable that he concealed his Islam and committed these sins out of stinginess with his kingship... And from the speech of al-Harith ibn 'Amr al-Fihri: اللهُمَّ إِنْ كانَ هذا هُوَ الْحَقَّ مِنْ عِنْدِكَ (O Allah, if this is indeed the truth from You) that Banu Hashim will inherit Heraclius (هرقلا) after Heraclius, he meant that Banu Hashim would inherit kingship (ملكاً) after kingship.

(٣٥) وَما كانَ صَلاتُهُمْ عِنْدَ الْبَيْتِ إِلَّا مُكاءً ** whistling (صفيراً) ** وَتَصْدِيَةً ** clapping (تصفيقاً). Meaning: they placed whistling (المكاء) and clapping (التصدية) in place of the prayer (الصلوة). In al-Ma'ani and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), He said: It is whistling (التصفير) and clapping (التصفيق). In al-Uyun, narrated from Imam al-Rida (peace be upon him): Mecca (مكّة) was named Mecca (مكّة) (1) because people used to whistle (يمكون) in it. And it was said of the one who intended to go there, "He has whistled (قد مكا)" (2). That is the saying of Allah, the Exalted: وَما كانَ صَلاتُهُمْ عِنْدَ الْبَيْتِ إِلَّا مُكاءً وَتَصْدِيَةً. So, al-Muka' (المكاء) is whistling (الصفير), and al-Tasdiqah (التصديقة) is the clapping of the hands (تصفيق اليدين). It is said that they used to circumambulate the House naked, interlocking their fingers, whistling therein, and clapping. They used to do this when the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم recited during his prayer, thereby confusing Him. In al-Majma', it is narrated: When the Prophet صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم prayed in the Sacred Mosque (المسجد الحرام), two men from Banu 'Abd al-Dar would stand on His right and whistle (فيصفّران), and two men would stand on His left and clap with their hands (فيصفِقان بأيديهما), thereby confusing His prayer. So, Allah killed them all at Badr. ** فَذُوقُوا الْعَذابَ ** means the killing and captivity on the Day of Badr, or the punishment of the Fire in the Hereafter, بِما كُنْتُمْ تَكْفُرُونَ (is) because of your disbelief (بسبَب كفركم).

** وَما كانَ صَلاتُهُمْ عِنْدَ الْبَيْتِ إِلَّا مُكاءً وَتَصْدِيَةً فَذُوقُوا الْعَذابَ بِما كُنْتُمْ تَكْفُرُونَ ** (35) Their prayer at the House was nothing but whistling and clapping. So taste the punishment because of what you used to disbelieve.

Al-Qummi (said) that this ** الْآيَةَ ** is connected (معطوفة) to His saying: ** وَإِذْ يَمْكُرُ بِكَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا **, as we narrated from him there.

(٣٦) إِنَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا يُنْفِقُونَ أَمْوالَهُمْ لِيَصُدُّوا عَنْ سَبِيلِ اللهِ فَسَيُنْفِقُونَها ثُمَّ تَكُونُ عَلَيْهِمْ حَسْرَةً ثُمَّ يُغْلَبُونَ. (36) Indeed, those who are faithless spend their wealth to bar [people] from the way of Allah. They will soon spend it, then it will be a regret for them, then they will be overcome.

Al-Qummi said: It was revealed concerning Quraysh when Ḍamḍam reached them and informed them of the news of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم regarding the pursuit of the caravan (العير). So they brought out their wealth, loaded up, spent, and went out to fight the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم at Badr. They were killed and ended up in the Fire, and what they spent became a regret (حسرة) for them.

I say: The naming of some of the hypocrites (المنافقين) in the story of Badr has already passed.

وَالَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا إِلى جَهَنَّمَ يُحْشَرُونَ ** (and those who are faithless will be mustered toward hell.) They will be driven (يُسَاقُونَ).

(٣٧) لِيَمِيزَ اللهُ الْخَبِيثَ مِنَ الطَّيِّبِ ** (37) That Allah may separate the bad from the good the disbeliever from the believer, and the righteous from the corrupt. وَيَجْعَلَ الْخَبِيثَ بَعْضَهُ عَلى بَعْضٍ فَيَرْكُمَهُ جَمِيعاً ** and put the bad, one part of it upon another, and heap it up all together, meaning, He will gather it and join some of it to some others فَيَجْعَلَهُ فِي جَهَنَّمَ ** and cast it into hell. all of it. أُولئِكَ هُمُ الْخاسِرُونَ ** It is they who are the losers. those who are complete in loss.

In al-Ilal, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) in a tradition: Indeed, when Allah, the Glorified, desired to create the clay (طينة) of the believer, He mixed it with the clay (طينة) of the disbeliever. Thus, any evil deed (سيّئة) the believer performs is only due to that mixture (المزاج). Likewise, when He desired to create the clay (طينة) of the disbeliever, He mixed it with the clay (طينة) of the believer. Thus, any good deed (حَسَنة) the disbeliever performs is only due to that mixture (المزاج), or words to this effect. He (peace be upon him) said: "When the Day of Resurrection comes, Allah will extract from the hostile enemy (الناصب) the origin (سنخ), the mixture (مزاج), the clay (طينة), the essence (جوهر), and the element (عنصر) of the believer, along with all his righteous deeds, and return it to the believer. And Allah, the Exalted, will extract from the believer the origin (سنخ), the mixture (مزاج), the clay (طينة), the essence (جوهر), and the element (عنصر) of the hostile enemy (الناصب), along with all his bad, rejected deeds, and return it to the hostile enemy (الناصب). This is out of His justice, exalted is His Majesty and sanctified are His Names. And He will say to the hostile enemy (الناصب): 'There is no injustice upon you. These wicked deeds are from your clay (طينتك) and your mixture (مزاجك), and you are more deserving of them. And these righteous deeds are from the clay (طينة) and mixture (مزاج) of the believer, and he is more deserving of them. There is no injustice today.' إِنَّ اللهَ سَرِيعُ الْحِسابِ* ** Indeed Allah is swift at reckoning. Then he (peace be upon him) said: "Shall I give you more regarding this meaning from the Quran? Does not Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, say: الْخَبِيثاتُ لِلْخَبِيثِينَ وَالْخَبِيثُونَ لِلْخَبِيثاتِ وَالطَّيِّباتُ لِلطَّيِّبِينَ وَالطَّيِّبُونَ لِلطَّيِّباتِ أُولئِكَ مُبَرَّؤُنَ مِمَّا يَقُولُونَ لَهُمْ مَغْفِرَةٌ وَرِزْقٌ كَرِيمٌ ** Bad women are for bad men, and bad men are for bad women, and good women are for good men, and good men are for good women. These are absolved of what they say. For them is forgiveness and a noble provision. And He, the Mighty and Majestic, said: وَالَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا إِلى جَهَنَّمَ يُحْشَرُونَ لِيَمِيزَ اللهُ الْخَبِيثَ مِنَ الطَّيِّبِ وَيَجْعَلَ الْخَبِيثَ بَعْضَهُ عَلى بَعْضٍ فَيَرْكُمَهُ جَمِيعاً فَيَجْعَلَهُ فِي جَهَنَّمَ أُولئِكَ هُمُ الْخاسِرُونَ ** But those who disbelieve will be mustered toward hell, that Allah may separate the bad from the good and put the bad, one part of it upon another, and heap it up all together, and cast it into hell. It is they who are the losers. We have intended the complete and proper version of this tradition and explained it in our book called al-Wafi. Whoever desires it should seek it there.

(٣٨) قُلْ لِلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا إِنْ يَنْتَهُوا ** (38) Say to the faithless, If they desist, from disbelief and enmity towards the Messenger يُغْفَرْ لَهُمْ ما قَدْ سَلَفَ ** they shall be forgiven for what is past, of their sins وَإِنْ يَعُودُوا ** but if they revert, to fighting Him فَقَدْ مَضَتْ سُنَّتُ الْأَوَّلِينَ ** the precedent of the ancients has already passed. those who formed factions against the Prophets (الأنبياء) resulting in destruction, just as happened to the people of Badr (أهل بدر), so let them expect the like of that.

And al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): that a man said to him: "I was a worker (عاملاً) for Banu Umayyah (بني أميّة) and I acquired a great deal of wealth (مالاً كثيراً). I thought that this was not lawful (لا يحلّ لي) for me, so I asked about it." "So it was said to me that your family (أهلك), your wealth (مالك), and everything you possess is unlawful (حرام)." He (peace be upon him) said: "It is not as they told you." The man asked: "Do I have repentance (توبة)?" He (peace be upon him) said: "Yes, your repentance is in the Book of Allah: قُلْ لِلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا إِنْ يَنْتَهُوا يُغْفَرْ لَهُمْ ما قَدْ سَلَفَ (Say to the faithless, If they desist, they shall be forgiven for what is past)."

(٣٩) وَقاتِلُوهُمْ حَتَّى لا تَكُونَ فِتْنَةٌ ** (39) And fight them until there is no more temptation There is no shirk (شرك) (polytheism) among them. Al-Qummi said: meaning kufr (كفر) (disbelief). He said: And this verse abrogates His saying: كُفُّوا أَيْدِيَكُمْ ** (restrain your hands) and His saying: وَدَعْ أَذاهُمْ ** (and disregard their annoyance). وَيَكُونَ الدِّينُ كُلُّهُ لِلَّهِ ** (and the religion is entirely for Allah). And the false religions (الأديان الباطلة) will vanish from them. In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): The interpretation (تأويل) of this verse has not yet come, for the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم granted them a concession due to his need and the need of his companions. But if its interpretation (تأويلها) were to come, it would not be accepted from them; rather, they would be fought until Allah is declared One (يُوحد الله) and until there is no shirk (شرك). And in al-Majma' and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The interpretation (تأويل) of this verse has not come. If our Qa'im (قائمنا) were to rise, those who reach his time will see what the interpretation (تأويل) of this verse will be. And the religion of Muhammad صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم will surely reach wherever the night reaches, until there is no polytheist (مشرك) upon the face of the earth, just as Allah the Almighty said: يَعْبُدُونَنِي لا يُشْرِكُونَ بِي شَيْئاً (They worship Me, not associating anything with Me) فَإِنِ انْتَهَوْا ** (But if they cease) from al-kufr (الكفر) (disbelief), فَإِنَّ اللهَ بِما يَعْمَلُونَ بَصِيرٌ ** (then Allah is watchful of what they do). So He will reward them for ceasing from it and for their submission (إسلامهم).

(٤٠) وَإِنْ تَوَلَّوْا ** (40) But if they turn their backs, and do not desist (وَلَمْ يَنْتَهُوا). فَاعْلَمُوا أَنَّ اللهَ مَوْلاكُمْ ** then know that Allah is your Master: He is your Helper (نَاصِرُكُمْ). So trust in Him and do not care about their enmity (مُعَادَاتِهِمْ). نِعْمَ الْمَوْلى ** an excellent Master He does not abandon the one who takes Him as a Master (تَوَلَّاهُ). وَنِعْمَ النَّصِيرُ ** and an excellent Helper! The one whom He helps is not overcome.

(٤١) وَاعْلَمُوا أَنَّما غَنِمْتُمْ مِنْ شَيْءٍ قيل أي الذي أخذتموه من الكفّار قهراً. (And know that whatever you gain) In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "By Allah, it is the benefit (الإِفادة) gained day by day." I say: meaning the acquisition (استفادة) of wealth from whatever direction it may come. فَأَنَّ لِلَّهِ خُمُسَهُ وَلِلرَّسُولِ وَلِذِي الْقُرْبى وَالْيَتامى وَالْمَساكِينِ وَابْنِ السَّبِيلِ. (Then a fifth of it belongs to Allah and the Apostle, and to the near relatives, the orphans, the needy and the traveller.) In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Indeed, dhū al-qurbā (ذُو الْقُرْبَى) are the relatives of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, and the fifth (الخمس) is for the Messenger and for us. And al-Ayyashi narrated the like of it from one of the two Imams (peace be upon them both), and he added: He was asked if the orphans (الْيَتَامَى) and the needy and the traveller were among them. He said: "Yes." In al-Kafi and al-Tahdhib, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): "By Allah, He meant us by dhū al-qurbā (ذِي الْقُرْبَى), those whom Allah joined with Himself and His Messenger when He said: ما أَفاءَ اللهُ عَلى رَسُولِهِ مِنْ أَهْلِ الْقُرى فَلِلَّهِ وَلِلرَّسُولِ وَلِذِي الْقُرْبى وَالْيَتامى وَالْمَساكِينِ وَابْنِ السَّبِيلِ ** specifically from among us." (What Allah has restored to His Apostle from the people of the townships belongs to Allah and the Apostle, and to the near relatives, the orphans, the needy and the traveller) He (peace be upon him) said: "And He did not grant us a share in the portion of al-sadaqah (الصدقة). Allah honored His Prophet and honored us by not feeding us the filth (أوساخ) of what is in the hands of the people." In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Rida (peace be upon him): He was asked about this Ayah, and it was said to him: "What belongs to Allah, then to whom does it belong?" He replied: "To the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. And what belongs to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, that belongs to the Imam." It was said to him: "Do you see if one category of recipients is greater in number and another category is fewer, what should be done with it?" He said: "That is up to the Imam. Do you not see how the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم used to act? He only used to give according to what He deemed appropriate. Likewise, the Imam." In al-Faqih, al-Tahdhib, and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "As for the fifth (خمس) belonging to Allah, it is for the Messenger, who places it in the way of Allah (سبيل الله). As for the fifth belonging to the Messenger, it is for his relatives. And the fifth of dhawī al-qurbā (ذَوِي الْقُرْبَى)—they are his relatives. And the orphans (اليتامى) are the orphans of his Household (أهل بيته). So, these four shares (الأسهم) are designated for them. As for the needy (المساكين) and the traveller (ابن السبيل), you know that we do not consume al-sadaqah (الصدقة) and it is not lawful for us, so it is for the needy and the travellers." In al-Tahdhib, narrated from one of the two Imams (peace be upon them both): "The fifth (خمس) belonging to Allah is for the Imam, and the fifth belonging to the Messenger is for the Imam, and the fifth of dhī al-qurbā (ذِي الْقُرْبَى) is for the relatives of the Messenger and the Imam. And the orphans (اليتامى) are the orphans of the Messenger, and the needy (المساكين) are from among them, so it does not go out from them to others." And al-Qummi (said): They are specifically the orphans of the Household of Muhammad (may the blessings of Allah be upon them), their needy, and their travellers. The fifth (الخمس) is taken out of the spoils (الغنيمة) and divided into six shares (أسهم): a share for Allah, a share for the Messenger of Allah, and a share for the Imam. The share of Allah and the share of the Messenger are inherited by the Imam, so the Imam receives three shares out of six. The three remaining shares are for the orphans of the Household of the Messenger (may the blessings of Allah be upon them), their needy, and their travellers. The Imam alone received three shares of the fifth because Allah the Almighty obligated him with what He obligated the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: the upbringing of orphans, the maintenance (مؤن) of the Muslims, the settlement of their debts, and transporting them for Hajj (الحجّ) and Jihad (الجهاد). This is based on the saying of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم when it was revealed to him: النَّبِيُّ أَوْلى بِالْمُؤْمِنِينَ (The Prophet is closer to the believers) مِنْ أَنْفُسِهِمْ ** and He is a father to them. When Allah made Him a father to the believers, He became responsible for them just as a father is responsible for his child. He then said: "Whoever leaves behind wealth, it is for his heirs; and whoever leaves behind a debt or dependents (ضياعاً), then it is upon me and to me." Thus, the Imam became responsible for what the Messenger صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم was responsible for, and that is why he received three shares of the fifth. إِنْ كُنْتُمْ آمَنْتُمْ بِاللهِ ** is related to an omitted phrase, meaning: If you have faith in Allah, then know that the fifth (الخمس) of the spoils (الغنيمة) must be used to seek nearness (to Him), so cut off your greed for it and be content with the four-fifths. وَما أَنْزَلْنا ** (meaning: and by what We sent down) عَلى عَبْدِنا ** Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, such as the signs (الآيات), the angels, and the victory, يَوْمَ الْفُرْقانِ ** which is the Day of Badr, for on that day He differentiated between truth and falsehood, يَوْمَ الْتَقَى (1) الْجَمْعانِ the Muslims and the disbelievers. (and what We sent down to Our servant on the Day of the Criterion, the day when the two hosts met) In al-Khisal, in the narration concerning the ritual baths (الأغسال), narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): The night when the two hosts met is the night of Badr. **وَاللهُ عَلى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ ** Thus, He has the power to grant victory to the few over the many and to provide aid with angels. (and Allah has power over all things.)

(٤٢) إِذْ أَنْتُمْ بِالْعُدْوَةِ الدُّنْيا (42) When you were on the nearer side [of the valley] from the City (المدينة), being a substitute (بدل) for يَوْمَ الْفُرْقانِ (the Day of the Criterion). And العدوة (al-ʿudwah) is vocalized with three possibilities (مثلثة) and means the bank (شطّ) of the valley (الوادي). وَهُمْ بِالْعُدْوَةِ الْقُصْوى and they were on the farther side the farthest (البعدى) from the City (المدينة), being the feminine form (تأنيث) of الأقصى (al-aqṣā). Al-Qummi said: He means Quraysh, where they encamped at the Yemeni side (العدوة اليمانية), while the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم encamped at the Syrian side (العدوة الشامية). And العِدوة (al-ʿidwah) was read with a kasrah on the ʿayn. وَالرَّكْبُ and the caravan [was] Al-Qummi said: He means the trade caravan (العير) that escaped. And Al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام: He means Abu Sufyan and his companions. I say: And the two interpretations are unified, for Abu Sufyan was with the trade caravan (العير). أَسْفَلَ مِنْكُمْ below you. in a place lower than your location, leading the trade caravan (العير) along the coast (الساحل). The benefit (الفائدة) in mentioning these locations (المواطن) is to inform about the situation (الحالة) which indicates the strength of the polytheists (المشركين) and the weakness of the Muslims (المسلمين), and that their victory over such a situation is a Divine matter (أمر الهيّ) that is not facilitated except through His power (حوله) and strength (قوّته). This is because the farther side (العدوة القصوى) had water, but there was no water بِالْعُدْوَةِ الدُّنْيا on the nearer side. And it was soft (رخوة), such that feet would sink into it (تسوخ فيها الأرجل). And the trade caravan (العير) was behind their backs, despite their large numbers. Thus, the protection of it doubled their zeal (حميّتهم) and compelled them not to leave their positions (مواطنهم) and to exert their utmost effort (نهاية نجدتهم). In this is a depiction of what Allah planned concerning the matter of the Battle of Badr (وقعة بدر). وَلَوْ تَواعَدْتُمْ لَاخْتَلَفْتُمْ فِي الْمِيعادِ Had you made an appointment [to fight], you would have failed to keep the tryst. That is, had you and they made an appointment for battle, and then you knew your situation and their situation, some of you would have disagreed with others. Your small number (قلتكم) would have deterred you from fulfilling the appointment, and the fear (الرعب) in their hearts would have deterred them. Thus, the fulfillment that Allah facilitated would not have occurred for you. وَلكِنْ لِيَقْضِيَ اللهُ أَمْراً كانَ مَفْعُولاً But [it was] that Allah might carry out a matter that was bound to be done, which was necessary (واجباً) to be done, such as strengthening His religion (إعزاز دينه), exalting His word (كلمته), helping His allies (أوليائه), and conquering His enemies (أعدائه). لِيَهْلِكَ مَنْ هَلَكَ عَنْ بَيِّنَةٍ by seeing it clearly (عيانها), وَيَحْيى مَنْ حَيَّ عَنْ بَيِّنَةٍ by witnessing it (شاهدها). Al-Qummi said: He knows that Allah helped those who remained. And it was said: So that the disbelief of the one who disbelieved and the faith of the one who believed might proceed from the clarity of manifest proof (وضوح بيّنة) and the establishment of argument (قيام حجّة). And حَيِيَ (ḥayiya) was read by undoing the assimilation (فكّ الإِدغام). وَإِنَّ اللهَ لَسَمِيعٌ عَلِيمٌ Indeed Allah is all-hearing, all-knowing. He knows how He manages your affairs.

(١) Al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Baqir عليه السلام: On the nineteenth of the month of Ramadan, the two hosts (الجمعان) meet. It was asked: What is the meaning of 'the two hosts meet'? He said: In it, He gathers what He wills regarding its advancement and delay, according to His will and decree. "From him, may Allah have mercy on him."

(٤٣) إِذْ يُرِيكَهُمُ اللهُ فِي مَنامِكَ قَلِيلاً (When Allah showed them to you as few in your sleep) So that you might inform your companions of it, and it would be a confirmation for them and an encouragement against their enemy. وَلَوْ أَراكَهُمْ كَثِيراً لَفَشِلْتُمْ (and had He shown them to you as many, you would have lost heart) meaning, you would have become cowardly. وَلَتَنازَعْتُمْ فِي الْأَمْرِ (and disputed about the matter) meaning, the matter of fighting, and your opinions would have differed between steadfastness (الثبات) and flight (الفرار). وَلكِنَّ اللهَ سَلَّمَ But Allah granted safety (سَلَّمَ). He bestowed the blessing of safety (السلامَة) from losing heart (الفشل) and disputing (التنازع). إِنَّهُ عَلِيمٌ بِذاتِ الصُّدُورِ (Indeed, He knows well what is in the breasts.) He knows what will be in them and what changes their states, such as boldness (الجرأة) and cowardice (الجبن).

In al-Qummi, the address is to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, but the meaning is for his companions. Allah showed them (Quraysh) in their sleep as being few. وَلَوْ أَراكَهُمْ كَثِيراً (and had He shown them to you as many) they would have been terrified.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir عليه السلام: On the Day of Badr, Iblis was making the Muslims appear few in the eyes of the disbelievers, and making the disbelievers appear many in the eyes of the people. So Gabriel (جبرئيل) attacked him with the sword, and he fled from Him (Gabriel), saying: "O Gabriel (جبرئيل), I am granted respite (مؤجّل)," until he fell into the sea. It was asked: "Why does he fear, seeing that he is granted respite (مؤجّل)?" He (Imam al-Baqir عليه السلام) said: "Lest some of his limbs be severed."

وَإِذْ يُرِيكُمُوهُمْ إِذِ الْتَقَيْتُمْ فِي أَعْيُنِكُمْ قَلِيلاً ** (And when He showed them to you, when you met, as few in your eyes) As a confirmation of the vision of the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم and as a means of strengthening you. In al-Jawami', narrated from Ibn Mas'ud: "They were indeed made few in our eyes, so much so that I said to a man next to me, 'Do you see them as seventy?' He said, 'I see them as a hundred.' Then we captured a man from among them, and we asked, 'How many were you?' He said, 'A thousand.'" وَيُقَلِّلُكُمْ فِي أَعْيُنِهِمْ ** (and made you few in their eyes) until one of them said, "They are merely enough for a meal of a camel (أكلة جزور)." And Abu Jahl said, "They are nothing but enough for a meal of a head (أكلة رأس). If we were to send our slaves against them, they would seize them easily (أخذاً باليد)," as was mentioned previously in the story. He only made them few in their eyes so that they would dare to attack them before the encounter. Then He made them numerous in their eyes after the encounter, so that the multitude would surprise them, causing them to fear (فَيَهابُوا) and their strength (شوكتهم) to diminish when they saw what was not in their calculation. And this is among the great signs (آيات) of that battle and the wonders of Allah's power therein. For although sight may sometimes perceive the many as few and the few as many, it is not in this manner nor to this extent. لِيَقْضِيَ اللهُ أَمْراً كانَ مَفْعُولاً وَإِلَى اللهِ تُرْجَعُ الْأُمُورُ. (so that Allah may carry out a matter that was bound to be fulfilled, and to Allah all matters are returned.)

(٤٥) يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا إِذا لَقِيتُمْ فِئَةً ** (O you who have faith, when you encounter a host) When you fight a disbelieving or rebellious group, and the encounter (اللقاء) is what is predominant in fighting. فَاثْبُتُوا ** (Then stand firm) for their fighting, and do not disperse. وَاذْكُرُوا اللهَ كَثِيراً (And remember Allah frequently) in the places of war, supplicating to Him, seeking strength through His remembrance, anticipating His victory. **لَعَلَّكُمْ تُفْلِحُونَ ** (So that you may be felicitous)—you achieve your desired outcome of victory and reward.

It is said that in this is an alert that the servant ought not to be distracted by anything from the remembrance of Allah the Almighty, and that he should seek refuge in Him during hardships, and turn to Him with his whole being (بشراشرة) (١), with a clear mind, confident that His kindness (لطفه) will not abandon him in any circumstance.

(٤٦) وَأَطِيعُوا اللهَ وَرَسُولَهُ وَلا تَنازَعُوا ** (46) and obey Allah and His Apostle, and do not dispute, by differing in opinions (الآراء), as you did at Badr and Uhud, فَتَفْشَلُوا ** lest you lose heart so that you become weak (تضعفوا) regarding fighting your enemy, وَتَذْهَبَ رِيحُكُمْ ** and your power vanish. your state (دولتكم). The state (الدولة) is likened to the wind (الريح) in the execution of its command (نفوذ أمرها) and its blowing (وهبوبها). It is said, "The wind (ريح) of so-and-so has blown (هبّت)" when his command (أمره) is executed (نفذ). And it was said: There was never any victory (نصر) except through a wind (ريح) that Allah sends forth (يبعثها). And in the Prophetic Hadith صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم: "I was granted victory (نُصِرْت) by the East wind (الصَّبا), and 'Ad was destroyed (أهلكت) by the West wind (الدّبور)." وَاصْبِرُوا إِنَّ اللهَ مَعَ الصَّابِرِينَ ** And be patient; indeed Allah is with the patient. through protection (الكلاءة) and victory (النصر).

(٤٧) وَلا تَكُونُوا كَالَّذِينَ خَرَجُوا مِنْ دِيارِهِمْ ** (47) Do not be like those who left their homes meaning the people of Mecca when they left it to protect the caravan (العير), boastfully (بَطَراً), proudly (فخراً), and insolently (أشراً). وَرِئاءَ النَّاسِ ** and boastfully before the people, so that people might praise them for courage and generosity. That was because when they reached Juhfah (2) and the messenger of Abu Sufyan came to them (saying) "Go back, for your caravan has been saved," Abu Jahl refused and said: "Until we arrive at Badr, where we shall drink wines (الخمور), and the singing girls (القيان) shall play music (تَعْزِف) (1) for us, and we shall feed those Arabs who attend us there." That was their boastfulness (بَطَر) and showing off (رِئاء). So they arrived there, and they were made to drink the cup of death (كأس الحمام) instead of wine, and the wailing women (النوائح) wailed over them instead of the singing girls (القيان). Thus, Allah forbade the believers from being like them, boastful (بَطِرين) and showing off (مُرائين). وَيَصُدُّونَ عَنْ سَبِيلِ اللهِ وَاللهُ بِما يَعْمَلُونَ مُحِيطٌ. and bar [people] from the way of Allah. And Allah comprehends what they do.

(1) Al-Sharāshir (الشراشر) means burdens (الأثقال). The singular is sharsharah (شرشره). It is said: "He cast his sharāshir (شراشره) upon him," meaning his soul (نفسه) out of eagerness (حرصاً) and love (محبّة).

(2) And Juhfah (جحفة) is a place between Mecca and Medina, and it is the mīqāt (ميقات) for the people of Syria (أهل الشام). Its name used to be Mahya’ah (مهيعة), but the flood (السّيل) swept away (أجحف) its people, so it was named Juhfah (جحفة).

The Almighty's saying: (٤٨) وَإِذْ زَيَّنَ لَهُمُ الشَّيْطانُ أَعْمالَهُمْ ** (And when Satan made their deeds fair-seeming to them) in their enmity towards the Messenger and other matters, by whispering to them, and [Satan] said: لا غالِبَ لَكُمُ الْيَوْمَ مِنَ النَّاسِ وَإِنِّي جارٌ لَكُمْ (There is no one among the people who can overcome you today, and I am your protector (مجيركم)). فَلَمَّا تَراءَتِ الْفِئَتانِ ** (But when the two hosts sighted each other)—the two parties met—نَكَصَ عَلى عَقِبَيْهِ ** (he retreated on his heels)—he retreated backward (القهقرى), and his plot failed, and what he had made them imagine—that he was their protector—became the cause of their destruction—وَقالَ إِنِّي بَرِيءٌ مِنْكُمْ إِنِّي أَرى ما لا تَرَوْنَ ** (and said, 'I am quit of you. I see what you do not see.')—meaning the armies of the angels—إِنِّي أَخافُ اللهَ ** (I fear Allah)—lest some harm befall me—وَاللهُ شَدِيدُ الْعِقابِ (and Allah is severe in retribution). An explanation for this verse has already passed in Surah Āl ‘Imrān concerning the story of Badr.

In al-Majma‘, narrated from al-Baqir and from al-Sadiq (peace be upon them both): When they met, Iblis was in the ranks of the polytheists, holding the hand of al-Harith ibn Hisham. Then نَكَصَ عَلى عَقِبَيْهِ ** (he retreated on his heels). Al-Harith said to him: "O Suraqah, do you abandon us in this state?" He replied: إِنِّي أَرى ما لا تَرَوْنَ ** ('I see what you do not see.'). Al-Harith said: "By Allah, you see nothing but spies from Yathrib!" So he pushed al-Harith in the chest, departed, and the people were routed. When they arrived in Mecca, the people said: "Suraqah caused the defeat." This reached Suraqah, and he said: "By Allah, I was not aware of your march until news of your defeat reached me." They said: "You came to us on such-and-such a day." So he swore to them. When they embraced Islam, they knew that it was Satan.

In al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sajjad (peace be upon him): When the people became thirsty on the Day of Badr, Ali (peace be upon him) went with the waterskin to draw water. While He was at the well (3), a strong wind came, and then it passed. He waited for as long as Allah willed, then another wind came, and then it passed, then a third came, which almost distracted Him while He was at the well (3). Then He sat until it passed. When He returned to the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم, He informed him of that. The Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "As for the first wind, in it was Jibra’il with a thousand angels. And the second, in it was Mika’il with a thousand angels. And the third, in it was Israfil with a thousand angels. And they greeted you with peace, and they are reinforcements for us. And they are the ones whom Iblis saw, so he نَكَصَ عَلى عَقِبَيْهِ ** (retreated on his heels), walking backward (القهقرى), when he said: إِنِّي أَرى ما لا تَرَوْنَ ** ('I see what you do not see,') [to the end of] the verse."

(٤٩) إِذْ يَقُولُ الْمُنافِقُونَ وَالَّذِينَ فِي قُلُوبِهِمْ مَرَضٌ غَرَّ هؤُلاءِ دِينُهُمْ وَمَنْ يَتَوَكَّلْ عَلَى اللهِ فَإِنَّ اللهَ عَزِيزٌ حَكِيمٌ ٤٩ (49) When the hypocrites and those in whose hearts is a sickness said, 'Their religion has deluded these,' but whoever puts his trust in Allah, then Allah is All-mighty, All-wise.

When the hypocrites and those in whose hearts is a sickness (the ones doubting Islam) say, Their religion has deluded these (meaning the Muslims), that is, they were deluded by their religion until, despite their small number, they exposed themselves to fighting a vast multitude. And whoever puts his trust in Allah (this is) a response to them, then Allah is All-mighty (Dominant (غالب)), Who grants victory to the weak over the strong and the few over the many, All-wise (حَكِيمٌ). He does, by His perfect wisdom, what the intellect deems improbable and is unable to comprehend. And an explanation for this Ayah and what follows it has already passed in the story of Badr.

(50) وَلَوْ تَرى ** (And if you were to see) meaning: If you were to see and witness, for لَوْ renders the present tense (المضارع) into the past tense (الماضي), contrary to إِنَّ. إِذْ يَتَوَفَّى الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا الْمَلائِكَةُ ** (when the angels take away the souls of the faithless) at Badr, and it has been recited as تَتَوَفَّى with the ت. يَضْرِبُونَ وُجُوهَهُمْ (striking their faces), meaning: what faced them, **وَأَدْبارَهُمْ ** (and their backs) meaning: what turned away.

In al-Ayyashi, narrated marfu'an: He only meant their buttocks (وَاسْتَاهَهُم). Verily, Allah is Generous; He uses euphemism (يَكْنِي).

وَذُوقُوا عَذابَ الْحَرِيقِ ** (and taste the punishment of the burning) And they [the angels] say: Taste the punishment of the Hereafter. And it was said: They had iron maces (مَقَامِع) with them, and whenever they struck, fire flared up from them.

In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم: A man said to Him: "I charged against a man from the polytheists, and I went to strike him, but his head flew off [before I could strike him]." He said: "The angels preceded you to him."

(٥١) ذلِكَ بِما قَدَّمَتْ أَيْدِيكُمْ ** (That is because of what your hands have sent ahead) due to what your hands earned of disbelief (الكفر) and acts of disobedience (المعاصي). وَأَنَّ اللهَ (١) لَيْسَ بِظَلَّامٍ لِلْعَبِيدِ ** (and that Allah is not tyrannical to the servants) meaning that Allah punishes the disbelievers (الكفّار) with justice, because He does not wrong His servants in their punishment. And the intensive form ظَلَّام (greatly unjust) is used to denote the abundance (للمتكثّر) for the sake of the servants (لأجل العبيد).

كَدَأْبِ آلِ فِرْعَوْنَ ** (Like the custom of Pharaoh’s clan) meaning, the custom (دأب) of these people is like the custom (دأب) of Pharaoh’s clan. And their custom (دأب), their habit (عادتهم), and their action (عملهم) which they persisted (دأبوا) in—meaning, they continued (داوموا) it. وَالَّذِينَ مِنْ قَبْلِهِمْ ** (and those who were before them.) from before Pharaoh’s clan. كَفَرُوا بِآياتِ اللهِ ** (They defied the signs of Allah.) This is an explanation of their custom (دأب). فَأَخَذَهُمُ اللهُ بِذُنُوبِهِمْ ** (So Allah seized them for their sins.) just as He seized these people. **إِنَّ اللهَ قَوِيٌّ شَدِيدُ الْعِقابِ ** (Indeed, Allah is all-strong, severe in retribution.) Nothing can overcome Him in repelling it.

footnote (١) And in this, there is clear evidence (*دلالة واضحة*) for the invalidity (*بطلان*) of the doctrine (*مذهب*) of the Determinists (*المجبّرة*)—that He creates disbelief (*الكفر*) and then punishes for it, and that it is permissible (*يجوز*) for Him to punish without sin (*ذنب*) or to seize someone for the sin (*ذنب*) of another. Because this is the utmost injustice (*غاية الظّلم*). And His exalted Name has emphasized the negation of injustice (*نفي الظّلم*) from Himself by His saying: **لَيْسَ بِظَلَّامٍ لِلْعَبِيدِ.** (and Allah is not tyrannical to the servants.)

(53) ذلِكَ ** is a reference to what befell them بِأَنَّ اللهَ ** because Allah was not one who changes—it is not proper according to His wisdom that He should change—a blessing He bestowed upon a people, replacing it with retribution (النّقمة), حَتَّى يُغَيِّرُوا ما بِأَنْفُسِهِمْ ** until they change their condition to a worse condition, such as the change of condition by Quraysh regarding maintaining kinship ties (صلة الرّحم) and refraining from opposing the signs (الآيات) and the Messengers, by showing enmity towards the Messenger and those who followed him among them, striving to shed their blood, denying the signs, and mocking them, and other things they innovated after the Prophetic mission (البعث).

ذلِكَ بِأَنَّ اللهَ لَمْ يَكُ مُغَيِّراً نِعْمَةً أَنْعَمَها عَلى قَوْمٍ حَتَّى يُغَيِّرُوا ما بِأَنْفُسِهِمْ وَأَنَّ اللهَ سَمِيعٌ عَلِيمٌ (53) (That is because Allah would never change a blessing that He has bestowed on a people unless they change what is in their own souls, and because Allah is all-hearing, all-knowing.)

وَأَنَّ اللهَ سَمِيعٌ ** of what they say عَلِيمٌ ** of what they do.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Indeed, Allah sent a Prophet from among His Prophets to his people and revealed to him: Say to your people that there is no people of a town or group of people who were obedient to Me, and prosperity (سرّاء) befell them therein, then they shifted from what I love to what I dislike, except that I shifted for them from what they love to what I dislike. And there is no people of a town or household who were disobedient to Me, and hardship (ضرّاء) befell them therein, then they shifted from what I dislike to what I love, except that I shifted for them from what they dislike to what they love. (The narration continues).

And narrated from him (peace be upon him): He says that my father used to say: Indeed, Allah decreed an absolute decree (قضاءً حتماً): He does not bestow a blessing upon the servant and then strip it away from him until the servant commits a sin by which he deserves the retribution (النّقمة).

(٥٤) كَدَأْبِ آلِ فِرْعَوْنَ وَالَّذِينَ مِنْ قَبْلِهِمْ كَذَّبُوا بِآياتِ رَبِّهِمْ فَأَهْلَكْناهُمْ بِذُنُوبِهِمْ وَأَغْرَقْنا آلَ فِرْعَوْنَ ** (54) (It is) like the custom of Pharaoh’s clan and those who were before them: they denied the signs of their Lord, so We destroyed them for their sins, and We drowned Pharaoh’s clan. A repetition for emphasis (للتّأكيد). And in His saying بِآياتِ رَبِّهِمْ (the signs of their Lord) there is an added indication of ingratitude for blessings (كفران النّعم). And in the mention of drowning (الإغراق) there is a clarification of the seizing (الأخذ) due to sins (بالذّنوب). وَكُلٌ ** meaning, every one of the drowned ones of Pharaoh's clan (آل فرعون) and the slain ones of Quraysh كانُوا ظالِمِينَ ** They were unjust (ظالِمِينَ) to themselves (أنفسهم) through their disbelief (بكفرهم) and their acts of disobedience (ومعاصيهم).

(٥٥) The Almighty's saying: **إِنَّ شَرَّ الدَّوَابِّ عِنْدَ اللهِ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا ** (55) Indeed the worst of beasts in Allah’s sight are those who are faithless, They persisted in disbelief and became firmly rooted in it, so they do not believe, and no faith is expected from them.

Al-Qummi and Al-Ayyashi narrated from Al-Baqir (peace be upon him): It was revealed concerning Banu Umayyah, for they are the worst of Allah's creation. They are those who disbelieved in the inner meaning (بطن) of the Quran.

(٥٦) الَّذِينَ عاهَدْتَ مِنْهُمْ ثُمَّ يَنْقُضُونَ عَهْدَهُمْ فِي كُلِّ مَرَّةٍ ** (56) those with whom you made a treaty, then they break their treaty every time It was said they are the Jews of Banu Qurayzah. The Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم made a treaty with them that they would not assist an enemy against him, but they broke it by assisting the polytheists of Mecca with weapons. And they said, "We forgot." Then He made a treaty with them, and they broke it and assisted the Confederates (الأحزاب) against him on the Day of the Trench (يوم الخندق). And al-Qummi (القمّيّ) [said] they are his companions who fled on the Day of Uhud (يوم أُحد). وَهُمْ لا يَتَّقُونَ ** (and they do not fear [Allah]). They do not fear the consequence of treachery, nor do they care about the shame (العار) and the Fire (النار) that is in it.

(٥٧) فَإِمَّا تَثْقَفَنَّهُمْ ** (57) (If you come upon them) You encounter them and gain victory over them. فِي الْحَرْبِ فَشَرِّدْ بِهِمْ ** (in war, scatter them) So, drive away [others] from fighting you, and deter them from it by killing them and inflicting severe punishment upon them, [specifically] those who are behind them (مَنْ خَلْفَهُمْ)—those unbelievers (الكفرة) who follow them. And al-tashrīd (التشريد) is scattering amidst confusion (اضطراب). لَعَلَّهُمْ يَذَّكَّرُونَ ** (so that they may take admonition.) They take heed (يتعظُونَ).

(٥٨) **وَإِمَّا تَخافَنَّ مِنْ قَوْمٍ خِيانَةً فَانْبِذْ إِلَيْهِمْ عَلى سَواءٍ إِنَّ اللهَ لا يُحِبُّ الْخائِنِينَ ** (58) If you fear treachery from a people, repudiate the treaty with them in like manner. Indeed, Allah does not like the treacherous.

وَإِمَّا تَخافَنَّ مِنْ قَوْمٍ ** (And if you fear from a people): meaning, those who have a treaty (معاهدين). خِيانَةً ** (treachery): meaning, the breaking of a covenant (نقض عهد) due to signs (أمارات) that appear to you. فَانْبِذْ إِلَيْهِمْ ** (repudiate the treaty with them): meaning, cast their covenant back to them (فاطرح إليهم عهدهم). عَلى سَواءٍ ** (in like manner): meaning, upon a moderate, level path (عَلى طريق مقتصد مستوٍ) in hostility. This is by informing them of the breaking of the covenant with a manifest, uncovered notification (إخباراً ظاهراً مكشوفاً) so that it becomes clear to them that you have severed what was between you and them. Do not initiate fighting against them while they are under the illusion of the covenant (توّهُم العهد), for that would be treachery (خيانة). إِنَّ اللهَ لا يُحِبُّ الْخائِنِينَ ** (Indeed, Allah does not like the treacherous): so do not betray them by engaging them in battle without informing them of the repudiation (النّبذ).

Al-Qummi said: It was revealed concerning Mu'awiyah (may Allah curse him) when he betrayed the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him).

**وَلا يَحْسَبَنَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا سَبَقُوا إِنَّهُمْ لا يُعْجِزُونَ ** (59) Let the faithless not suppose that they have gotten ahead: they cannot frustrate [Him].

وَلا يَحْسَبَنَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا ** (And let not those who are faithless suppose): It was also read with the yā’ (وقرئ بالياءِ) [meaning yaḥsabanna] (that they have gotten ahead (سَبَقُوا), meaning, escaped from being overcome by Him). إِنَّهُمْ لا يُعْجِزُونَ ** (they cannot frustrate [Him]): They will not escape, nor will they find their pursuer incapable of reaching them. It was also read with fatḥ (وقرئ بالفتح) [of the alif in inna] meaning: because they (بمعنى لأنّهم).

**وَأَعِدُّوا لَهُمْ مَا اسْتَطَعْتُمْ مِنْ قُوَّةٍ وَمِنْ رِباطِ الْخَيْلِ تُرْهِبُونَ بِهِ عَدُوَّ اللهِ وَعَدُوَّكُمْ وَآخَرِينَ مِنْ دُونِهِمْ لا تَعْلَمُونَهُمُ اللهُ يَعْلَمُهُمْ وَما تُنْفِقُوا مِنْ شَيْءٍ فِي سَبِيلِ اللهِ يُوَفَّ إِلَيْكُمْ وَأَنْتُمْ لا تُظْلَمُونَ ** (60) Prepare against them whatever capability and cavalry you can muster, to overawe the enemy of Allah and your enemy, and others besides them, whom you do not know, but Allah knows them. And whatever you spend in the way of Allah shall be repaid to you in full, and you will not be wronged.

وَأَعِدُّوا ** (And prepare): O believers (أيّها المؤمنون). لَهُمْ ** (against them): meaning, against the faithless (للكفار). مَا اسْتَطَعْتُمْ مِنْ قُوَّةٍ ** (whatever capability you can muster): meaning, everything by which one gains strength in war (من كل ما يتقوّى به في الحرب).

In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated marfū‘an (raised chain), and from the general populace (العامّة) narrated from the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم): "Indeed, the capability (القوّة) is archery (الرمّي)."

And al-Ayyashi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "[It is] a sword and a shield (سيف وترس)."

And al-Qummi said: "[It is] the weapon (السّلاح)."

And in al-Faqih, narrated from him (peace be upon him): "Part of it is dyeing [the hair] with black (الخضاب بالسّواد)."

وَمِنْ رِباطِ الْخَيْلِ ** (and cavalry): And al-ribāṭ (الرّباط) is a name for the horses (الخيل) that are tethered (تربط) in the way of Allah (في سبيل الله). تُرْهِبُونَ بِهِ ** (to overawe [with] it): meaning, you frighten [with] it (تخوّفون به). It was also read with tashdīd (وقرئ بالتشديد). عَدُوَّ اللهِ وَعَدُوَّكُمْ ** (the enemy of Allah and your enemy): meaning, the faithless of Mecca (كفّار مكّة). وَآخَرِينَ مِنْ دُونِهِمْ ** (and others besides them): meaning, from others among the disbelievers (من غيرهم من الكفرة). لا تَعْلَمُونَهُمُ ** (whom you do not know): meaning, you do not recognize them individually (بأعيانهم) because they pray and fast. اللهُ يَعْلَمُهُمْ (but Allah knows them): He recognizes them because He is the One Who is aware of the secrets (المطّلع على الأسرار). وَما تُنْفِقُوا مِنْ شَيْءٍ فِي سَبِيلِ اللهِ يُوَفَّ إِلَيْكُمْ ** (And whatever you spend in the way of Allah shall be repaid to you in full): meaning, its recompense (جزاؤه). وَأَنْتُمْ لا تُظْلَمُونَ (and you will not be wronged): by the loss of the deed (تضييع العمل) or the reduction of the reward (نقص الثواب).

(61) وَإِنْ جَنَحُوا ** (If they incline) — meaning, they leaned — لِلسَّلْمِ ** (to peace) — meaning, to reconciliation (الصلح) and surrender (الاستسلام). And it was recited with kasrah (السِّلْم). فَاجْنَحْ لَها ** (then incline to it) — and make a covenant with them. The feminization of the pronoun (in لَها) is due to relating it to its opposite, which is war (الحرب). An explanation for this verse has already passed in the story of Badr.

And al-Qummi said that it is abrogated (منسوخة) by His saying: فَلا تَهِنُوا وَتَدْعُوا إِلَى السَّلْمِ وَأَنْتُمُ الْأَعْلَوْنَ ** (So do not falter and invite to peace while you have the upper hand). This verse وَإِنْ جَنَحُوا was revealed before the revelation of يَسْئَلُونَكَ عَنِ الْأَنْفالِ and before the war. And it was written at the end of the Surah after the conclusion of the accounts of Badr.

In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked: "What is السلام (al-Salam)?" He said: "Entering into our affair (أمرنا)."

وَتَوَكَّلْ عَلَى اللهِ ** (and rely on Allah) — and do not fear their deceit and their plotting (مكرهم), for Allah is your Protector (عاصمك) and sufficient for you against them. إِنَّهُ هُوَ السَّمِيعُ ** (Indeed, He is the All-hearing) of their sayings الْعَلِيمُ ** (the All-knowing) of their intentions (نيّاتهم).

**وَإِنْ جَنَحُوا لِلسَّلْمِ فَاجْنَحْ لَها وَتَوَكَّلْ عَلَى اللهِ إِنَّهُ هُوَ السَّمِيعُ الْعَلِيمُ (٦١) ** (61) But if they incline toward peace, then you too incline toward it, and rely on Allah. Indeed, He is the All-hearing, the All-knowing.

(٦٢) وَإِنْ يُرِيدُوا أَنْ يَخْدَعُوكَ ** (And if they intend to deceive you) regarding the peace treaty, by intending through it to deter your companions from fighting until their power strengthens, so they may attack you with it while you are unprepared. فَإِنَّ حَسْبَكَ اللهُ ** (then Allah is sufficient for you) Allah is your sufficient protector (مُحْسِبُكَ). Al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): These are people from Quraysh who were with him (the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم). هُوَ الَّذِي أَيَّدَكَ ** (He it is Who supported you) He strengthened you. بِنَصْرِهِ وَبِالْمُؤْمِنِينَ.** (with His help and with the believers.)

(٦٣) وَأَلَّفَ بَيْنَ قُلُوبِهِمْ ** (63) And He put affection between their hearts. until they became mutually loving and affectionate after the mutual hatred (التضاغن) and warfare (التحارب) that existed between them.

In al-Majma' and al-Qummi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): "They are the Ansar, and they are the Aws and the Khazraj."

And al-Qummi added: There was intense warfare and enmity between the Aws and the Khazraj during the Age of Ignorance (الجاهلية). Allah وَأَلَّفَ ** (put affection) بَيْنَ قُلُوبِهِمْ ** (between their hearts) and granted victory to His Prophet through them.

لَوْ أَنْفَقْتَ ما فِي الْأَرْضِ جَمِيعاً ما أَلَّفْتَ بَيْنَ قُلُوبِهِمْ ** If you had spent all that is in the earth, you could not have put affection between their hearts. meaning that their enmity reached a limit such that if a spender were to spend all the wealth on earth to reconcile their affairs (إصلاح ذات بينهم), he would not be able to achieve affection (الألفة) and reconciliation (الإصلاح). But Allah put affection between them through Islam by His perfect power (بقدرته البالغة), for He is the Owner of the hearts, turning them however He wills. إِنَّهُ عَزِيزٌ ** (Indeed He is all-mighty), possessing complete power (تامّ القدرة) and dominance (الغَلَبة); what He wills is not disobeyed by Him. **حَكِيمٌ ** (all-wise), knowing how He ought to do what He wills.

(٦٤) يا أَيُّهَا النَّبِيُّ حَسْبُكَ اللهُ He is sufficient for you وَمَنِ اتَّبَعَكَ مِنَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ It was said that it was revealed at al-Bayda' during the Battle of Badr, before the fighting.

يا أَيُّهَا النَّبِيُّ حَسْبُكَ اللهُ وَمَنِ اتَّبَعَكَ مِنَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ (64) (O Prophet! Allah is sufficient for you, and [for] the faithful who follow you.)

(٦٥) يا أَيُّهَا النَّبِيُّ حَرِّضِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ عَلَى الْقِتالِ Be persistent (بالغ) in encouraging them to fight.

يا أَيُّهَا النَّبِيُّ حَرِّضِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ عَلَى الْقِتالِ إِنْ يَكُنْ مِنْكُمْ عِشْرُونَ صابِرُونَ يَغْلِبُوا مِائَتَيْنِ وَإِنْ يَكُنْ مِنْكُمْ مِائَةٌ يَغْلِبُوا أَلْفاً مِنَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا بِأَنَّهُمْ قَوْمٌ لا يَفْقَهُونَ (65) (O Prophet! Urge the faithful to fight. If there be twenty patient men among you, they shall overcome two hundred; and if there be a hundred of you, they shall overcome a thousand of the faithless, for they are a people who do not understand.)

This is a promise (عِدَة) from Allah that the group (الجماعة) of the faithful, if they are patient, will overcome ten times their number of the disbelievers, through the support (تأييد) of Allah. And tukun (تكن) was recited with the tā' (التاء) [in the verb]. بِأَنَّهُمْ قَوْمٌ لا يَفْقَهُونَ ** (for they are a people who do not understand) due to the fact that the disbelievers are ignorant (جهلة) of Allah and the Last Day. They fight without seeking the reward (الثواب) [from Allah] and they do not stand firm with the steadfastness (ثبات) of the faithful who hope for the highest ranks (عوالي الدّرجات).

(66) الْآنَ خَفَّفَ اللهُ عَنْكُمْ وَعَلِمَ أَنَّ فِيكُمْ ضَعْفاً ** (66) Now Allah has lightened [the burden] for you, knowing that there is a weakness in you. And it was read with the fathah on the ḍād (ض). فَإِنْ يَكُنْ مِنْكُمْ مِائَةٌ صابِرَةٌ ** And it was read tukun (تكن) with the tā’ (ت). يَغْلِبُوا مِائَتَيْنِ وَإِنْ يَكُنْ مِنْكُمْ أَلْفٌ يَغْلِبُوا أَلْفَيْنِ بِإِذْنِ اللهِ So if there be a hundred of you patient, they shall overcome two hundred, and if there be a thousand of you, they shall overcome two thousand, by Allah’s leave. This verse (الآية) abrogates (ناسخة) what preceded it.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) in a long tradition (حديث طويل) where this verse was mentioned, He said: "The two men abrogated (نسخ) the ten."

And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): "Whoever flees from two men in battle, from the advance (الزّحف), has indeed fled from the advance (الزّحف). And whoever flees from three men in battle, from the advance (الزّحف), has not fled."

And al-Qummi narrated what is close to the meaning of the two traditions (الحديثين). It was said: They were few in number initially, so they were commanded with that (the 1:10 ratio). Then, when they became numerous, خَفَّفَ اللهُ ** (Allah lightened [the burden]) for them. وَاللهُ مَعَ الصَّابِرِينَ ** And Allah is with the patient. with victory (بالنصر) and assistance (والمعونة), so inevitably they will overcome.

(67) ما كانَ لِنَبِيٍّ أَنْ يَكُونَ لَهُ أَسْرى حَتَّى يُثْخِنَ فِي الْأَرْضِ ** (67) It is not for a prophet to have captives until he has thoroughly subdued the land. The killing becomes abundant and is intensified until disbelief (الكفر) is humiliated, its party diminishes, Islam is strengthened, and its people prevail.
