(It is derived) from (أثخنه) the disease (المرض) when it burdens him heavily (أثقله). تُرِيدُونَ عَرَضَ الدُّنْيا ** You desire the transient goods of the world, Its transient goods, by taking the ransom (الفداء). وَاللهُ يُرِيدُ الْآخِرَةَ ** but Allah desires the Hereafter. He desires for you the reward (ثواب) of the Hereafter. وَاللهُ عَزِيزٌ ** Allah is all-mighty, He makes His allies (أولياءه) victorious over His enemies (أعدائه). حَكِيمٌ ** all-wise. He knows what is fitting for every situation and specifies it with that. It was said that this was on the Day of Badr. Then, when the Muslims became numerous, (the verse) فَإِمَّا مَنًّا بَعْدُ وَإِمَّا فِداءً ** was revealed. And a clarification for this verse and what follows it has already passed in the story (قصة) of Badr.

(٦٨) لَوْ لا كِتابٌ مِنَ اللهِ سَبَقَ ** Meaning, a decree (حكم) from Him whose establishment in the Preserved Tablet (اللّوح) preceded, concerning the permissibility (اباحة) of the spoils (الغنائم) for you, لَمَسَّكُمْ (a painful punishment would have surely befallen you) — meaning, it would have reached you (لنالكم) — فِيما أَخَذْتُمْ ** (for what you took), for what you deemed lawful (استحللتم) before the permissibility (الإباحة) concerning the ransom (الفداءِ), عَذابٌ عَظِيمٌ. (68) Had there not been a decree from Allah that preceded, a great punishment would have surely befallen you for what you took.

(٦٩) فَكُلُوا مِمَّا غَنِمْتُمْ حَلالاً طَيِّباً وَاتَّقُوا اللهَ ** (69) So eat of what you have taken as spoils, as lawful and good, and be wary of Allah. (meaning: from the ransom) حَلالاً طَيِّباً وَاتَّقُوا اللهَ ** (meaning: regarding opposing Him) إِنَّ اللهَ غَفُورٌ ** (meaning: He has forgiven your sin) رَحِيمٌ ** (meaning: He has made lawful for you what you have taken).

(٧٠) يا أَيُّهَا النَّبِيُّ قُلْ لِمَنْ فِي أَيْدِيكُمْ مِنَ الْأَسْرى إِنْ يَعْلَمِ اللهُ فِي قُلُوبِكُمْ خَيْراً يُؤْتِكُمْ خَيْراً مِمَّا أُخِذَ مِنْكُمْ وَيَغْفِرْ لَكُمْ وَاللهُ غَفُورٌ رَحِيمٌ ** (70) O Prophet! Say to those of the captives who are in your hands, "If Allah knows any good in your hearts, He will give you better than what has been taken from you and forgive you, and Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful." يا أَيُّهَا النَّبِيُّ قُلْ لِمَنْ فِي أَيْدِيكُمْ مِنَ الْأَسْرى ** And it was read (الأسارى) إِنْ يَعْلَمِ اللهُ فِي قُلُوبِكُمْ خَيْراً ** (meaning: sincerity of creed and soundness of intention in faith) يُؤْتِكُمْ خَيْراً مِمَّا أُخِذَ مِنْكُمْ ** (meaning: from the ransom) وَيَغْفِرْ لَكُمْ وَاللهُ غَفُورٌ رَحِيمٌ A clarification for this verse has already passed in the story of Badr.

In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): that it was revealed concerning al-Abbas, Aqil, and Nawfal.

And He said that the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم forbade on the Day of Badr that anyone from Banu Hashim or Abu al-Bakhtari be killed. So they were taken captive. He sent Ali (peace be upon him) and said: "Look who is here from Banu Hashim." He (the narrator) said: Ali (peace be upon him) passed by Aqil ibn Abi Talib and turned away from him (variant reading حار خ ل عنه). So Aqil said to him: "O son of Ali's mother! By Allah, you certainly saw my place!" He (the narrator) said: So he returned to the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم and said: "This Abu al-Fadl is in the hand of so-and-so, and this Aqil is in the hand of so-and-so, and this Nawfal ibn al-Harith is in the hand of so-and-so." The Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم stood up until He reached Aqil and said to him: "O Abu Yazid, Abu Jahl has been killed." He (Aqil) said: "Then you will not be disputed over in Tihamah." He (the Prophet صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم) said: "If you have inflicted heavy losses upon the people (the enemy), otherwise, ride upon their shoulders (i.e., pursue them)." He (the narrator) said: Then al-Abbas was brought, and it was said to him: "Ransom yourself and ransom your two nephews." He said: "O Muhammad, do you leave me to beg Quraysh with my palm?" He (the Prophet صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم) said: "Give what you left with Umm al-Fadl and told her that if anything happened to me on this journey, she should spend it on your children and herself." He (al-Abbas) said to Him: "O son of my brother, who informed you of this?" He (the Prophet صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم) said: "Gabriel brought it to Me from Allah." He (al-Abbas) said, swearing an oath: "No one knew about this except me and her. I bear witness that you are the Messenger of Allah." He (the narrator) said: All the captives returned as polytheists except al-Abbas, Aqil, and Nawfal, and concerning them this verse was revealed: **قُلْ لِمَنْ فِي أَيْدِيكُمْ مِنَ الْأَسْرى ** the verse.

In Qurb al-Isnad, narrated from Imam al-Sajjad (peace be upon him), he said: Two hundred dirhams were brought to the Prophet صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم. He said: "O Abbas, spread out your cloak and take a portion of this wealth." So he spread out his cloak and took a portion of it. Then the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "This is from what Allah said: **إِنْ يَعْلَمِ اللهُ فِي قُلُوبِكُمْ خَيْراً يُؤْتِكُمْ خَيْراً مِمَّا أُخِذَ مِنْكُمْ ** the verse."

And al-Ayyashi narrated the like of it from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him).

(٧١) وَإِنْ يُرِيدُوا خِيانَتَكَ ** (71) And if they intend to betray you, (This means) breaking what they promised you. فَقَدْ خانُوا اللهَ مِنْ قَبْلُ** then they have already betrayed Allah before.

Al-Qummi: وَإِنْ يُرِيدُوا خِيانَتَكَ ** (And if they intend to betray you) concerning Ali, فَقَدْ خانُوا اللهَ مِنْ قَبْلُ concerning you, as passed in the story of Badr. So He enabled you against them on the Day of Badr. If they repeat the betrayal, He will enable [you] against them. وَاللهُ عَلِيمٌ حَكِيمٌ.** And Allah is All-Knowing, All-Wise.

(٧٢) إِنَّ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَهاجَرُوا ** (72) Indeed those who have believed and emigrated They departed from their homelands and their people out of love for Allah and His Messenger, and they are the Muhajirun (Emigrants) from Mecca to Medina. وَجاهَدُوا بِأَمْوالِهِمْ ** and struggled with their possessions so they spent them. وَأَنْفُسِهِمْ ** and their lives so they sacrificed them. فِي سَبِيلِ اللهِ وَالَّذِينَ آوَوْا وَنَصَرُوا ** in the way of Allah, and those who sheltered and helped [them]— And those who sheltered them in their homes and helped them against their enemies, and they are the Ansar (Helpers). **أُولئِكَ بَعْضُهُمْ أَوْلِياءُ بَعْضٍ ** these are the friends of one another. Meaning, some of them inherit from others in the matter of inheritance.

Al-Qummi said: When the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم emigrated to Medina, He established brotherhood (آخى) between the Muhajirun and the Muhajirun, between the Ansar and the Ansar, and between the Muhajirun and the Ansar. When a man died, his brother in faith would inherit from him and take the wealth, and he would receive what the deceased left behind, excluding his blood heirs (ورثته). Then, after [the Battle of] Badr, Allah revealed: النَّبِيُّ أَوْلى بِالْمُؤْمِنِينَ مِنْ أَنْفُسِهِمْ وَأَزْواجُهُ أُمَّهاتُهُمْ وَأُولُوا الْأَرْحامِ بَعْضُهُمْ أَوْلى بِبَعْضٍ فِي كِتابِ اللهِ ** The Prophet is closer to the faithful than their own souls, and his wives are their mothers. And the blood relatives are closer to one another in the Book of Allah ...the Ayah. So the verse of brotherhood was abrogated by بَعْضُهُمْ أَوْلى بِبَعْضٍ.** (some of them are closer to others).

In al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir عليه السلام: They used to inherit through the initial brotherhood (المؤاخاة) rather than through kinship (التّقارب), until that was abrogated by His saying: وَأُولُوا الْأَرْحامِ بَعْضُهُمْ أَوْلى بِبَعْضٍ And the blood relatives are closer to one another.

وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَلَمْ يُهاجِرُوا ما لَكُمْ مِنْ وَلايَتِهِمْ مِنْ شَيْءٍ حَتَّى يُهاجِرُوا ** As for those who have believed but have not emigrated, you have no concern with their guardianship until they emigrate. Meaning, [you have no concern] with inheriting from them. And wilāyatihim (ولايتهم) was read with a kasrah (vowel break below the wāw), likening it to the practice of a craft (العَمَل بالصناعة) such as writing (الكتابة) or governance (الأمارة), as if by taking charge (بتولية) of his companion, he is undertaking an action (عملاً).

Al-Ayyashi, narrated from both of them عَليهما السلام: The people of Mecca do not inherit from (لا يولّون) the people of Medina.

وَإِنِ اسْتَنْصَرُوكُمْ فِي الدِّينِ ** If they seek your help in the matter of religion, It was said that its meaning is: If the believers among you who have not emigrated seek help against the disbelievers. فَعَلَيْكُمُ النَّصْرُ إِلَّا عَلى قَوْمٍ بَيْنَكُمْ وَبَيْنَهُمْ مِيثاقٌ ** it is your duty to help them, except against a people with whom you have a treaty. Then it is not permissible for you to help them against that people. وَاللهُ بِما تَعْمَلُونَ بَصِيرٌ. And Allah is watchful of what you do.

(٧٣) وَالَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا بَعْضُهُمْ أَوْلِياءُ بَعْضٍ ** (73) But those who have disbelieved are allies of one another.**

The Muslims were forbidden from befriending (موالاة) the disbelievers and assisting them, even if they were relatives. And He made it obligatory that they leave them to ally with one another. إِلَّا تَفْعَلُوهُ ** (Meaning:) If you do not do what I commanded you regarding maintaining communication (التّواصل) among yourselves and allying with one another—even in inheritance, giving preference to the relationship of Islam over the relationship of kinship (القرابة)—and if you do not sever the ties (العلائق) between yourselves and the disbelievers, تَكُنْ فِتْنَةٌ فِي الْأَرْضِ وَفَسادٌ كَبِيرٌ (there will be turmoil in the land and great corruption). A great trial (فتنة عظيمة) and major corruption (مفسدة كبيرة) will occur therein, because as long as the Muslims are not united (يداً واحدة) against the people of polytheism (أهل الشرك), polytheism (الشّرك) will be manifest, and its adherents will dare to attack the people of Islam (أهل الإِسلام) and call them to disbelief (الكفر).

(٧٤) وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَهاجَرُوا وَجاهَدُوا فِي سَبِيلِ اللهِ وَالَّذِينَ آوَوْا وَنَصَرُوا أُولئِكَ هُمُ الْمُؤْمِنُونَ حَقًّا ** (74) And those who have believed and emigrated and struggled in the way of Allah, and those who gave [them] shelter and help—it is they who are truly the faithful. Because they actualized their faith through emigration (الهجرة), support (النّصرة), and detachment from family, wealth, and self for the sake of the religion (الدين). لَهُمْ مَغْفِرَةٌ وَرِزْقٌ كَرِيمٌ ** For them is forgiveness and a noble provision. There is no accountability (تبعة) for it, nor is there any obligation (منّة) in it.

(٧٥) وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا مِنْ بَعْدُ وَهاجَرُوا وَجاهَدُوا مَعَكُمْ ** (75) And those who believed afterwards and migrated and waged jihad along with you He means those who came later (اللاحقين) after those who came earlier (السابقين), like His saying: وَالَّذِينَ جاؤُ مِنْ بَعْدِهِمْ فَأُولئِكَ مِنْكُمْ ** (And those who came after them, they are of you). Meaning, they are part of your group, O Muhajirun and Ansar. Their ruling is your ruling regarding the obligation of loyalty (موالاتهم) and support (نصرتهم) for them, even if their faith and migration were delayed. وَأُولُوا الْأَرْحامِ ** (but the blood relatives)—meaning those with close kinship (أُولوا القربات)—بَعْضُهُمْ أَوْلى بِبَعْضٍ ** (are closer to one another). Some of them are more entitled to the inheritance of others than some others, and than those who are not relatives. This is an abrogation (نسخ) of inheriting based on migration (الهجرة) and support (النصرة), as its clarification preceded, فِي كِتابِ اللهِ ** (in the Book of Allah), in His written decree. And in it is an indication that whoever was closer to the deceased in lineage (variant reading: بالنّسب - by lineage) was more entitled to the inheritance.

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): When a freed slave (مولىً) of Ali (peace be upon him) died and left behind relatives (قرابته), Ali (peace be upon him) would not take anything from his inheritance, saying: أُولُوا الْأَرْحامِ بَعْضُهُمْ أَوْلى بِبَعْضٍ. (but the blood relatives are closer to one another).

And al-Qummi said: This verse abrogated His saying: وَالَّذِينَ عَقَدَتْ أَيْمانُكُمْ فَآتُوهُمْ نَصِيبَهُمْ. (and those with whom you have contracted a compact, give them their share).

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The Imamate will never return to two brothers after al-Hasan and al-Husayn (peace be upon them) ever. Rather, it proceeded from Ali ibn al-Husayn (peace be upon them) as Allah said: وَأُولُوا الْأَرْحامِ بَعْضُهُمْ أَوْلى بِبَعْضٍ فِي كِتابِ اللهِ ** (but the blood relatives are closer to one another in the Book of Allah). Thus, after Ali ibn al-Husayn (peace be upon them), it will only be among the descendants (الأعقاب) and the descendants of the descendants (أعقاب الأعقاب). إِنَّ اللهَ بِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ عَلِيمٌ ** (Indeed Allah is knowing of all things), regarding inheritances (المواريث) and other matters, and regarding the wisdom in linking it first to the relationship of Islam and mutual support (المظاهرة) (1), and secondly to the consideration of kinship (القرابة), and other than that.

The mention of the reward for reciting this Surah will come at the end of Surah al-Tawbah, if Allah the Almighty wills. And Allah is the Knower.

Surah al-Tawbah

And it is entirely Madani (مدنية). Some said that two verses are exceptions: لَقَدْ جاءَكُمْ رَسُولٌ مِنْ أَنْفُسِكُمْ (There has certainly come to you an Apostle from among yourselves) to the end of the Surah. Its number of verses is one hundred and twenty-nine verses. It was revealed in the ninth year of the Hijrah, and Mecca was conquered in the eighth year, and the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم performed the Farewell Pilgrimage (حجة الوداع) in the tenth year.

In al-Majma', narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful) was not revealed at the beginning of Surah Bara’ah because بِسْمِ اللهِ (In the Name of Allah) is for security (الأمان) and mercy (الرحمة), whereas Bara’ah was revealed for the removal of security and the sword (السيف).

And in it (al-Majma') and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): al-Anfal and Bara’ah are one (واحدة).

footnote (١) *المظاهرة* is conformity (*المطابقة*), mutual support (*المعاضدة*), assistance (*المعاونة*), and aid (*المساعدة*). Its origin is from the succession of two backs (*الظّهرين*), the joining of one to the other, and reliance upon it.
